question: what is tag 35 in FIX

ask.answer:
Tag 35 is MsgType.

Description: Defines message type ALWAYS THIRD FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)
Note: A "U" as the first character in the MsgType field (i.e. U, U2, etc) indicates that the message format is privately defined between the sender and receiver.

question: message broadcaster down
expected: Broadcaster Down / Alert Checklist KB article

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

Message Broadcaster Down

Step 1: Initial Status Check
Verify the status of the Message Broadcaster in Web Admin and open the Support mailbox simultaneously.
If the broadcaster is Active on us1-crd078081 (primary) or us1-crd088081 (backup), notify the MCM CRIMS Support – External chat that the alert has been received and the broadcaster is currently active. Also note that you will continue to monitor it's status. 
If the status is Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately and proceed to investigate further.
Message Broadcaster not running screenshot here

Step 2: Consistent Communication
Maintain regular communication with the external Moore chat, providing updates every few minutes if the broadcaster remains down, if the root cause is still being investigated, or what actions are being taken

Scenarios for Investigation:

Scenario 1: CTM Blocking
If you find blocking in the Support mailbox, identify whether the SPIDs with a Blocker value of 0 are CTM related or not (captured below). If they are proceed with the following:
Access the prod database using the CRDADMIN login and terminate the blocking SPIDs.
Escalate to the following team members if you do not have prod CRDADMIN credentials:
US: Andrew, Anuja
Dubai: Raed, Mike, Mark (Moore)


Scenario 2: Non-CTM Blocking
If the command is not a CTM command, escalate to Andrew and/or Moore contacts (Maher or Prasad) for confirmation on what the process is before killing it.
Commands that should not be killed without explicit approval include (but are not limited to):
TRIG_TS_ORDER_FIRM_POSITION
TS_ORDER_AU
Fn_Append_Allocation_Fields
Broadcast Queries
Add new examples here


Scenario 3: No Blocking but Long-Running Transactions
Navigate to Web Admin > Monitor and the Support mailbox to identify any existing long-running transactions.
If the transaction is broadcast query related, it should be killed.
Broadcast queries can be killed in the case of long-running transactions vs. a blocking because as a long running transaction, the process has not committed yet.
If unrelated, escalate to Andrew, Maher, or Prasad to get confirmation if the process can be terminated.


Scenario 4: No Blocking, No Long-Running Transactions
Review the Message Logs and Server Logs for any errors.
If errors are found, resolve appropriately (examples include restarting services, reboot middle tier, or adjust broadcast queries with Maher).
If no errors are found, initiate a failover to the backup middle tier.
Include steps here
Need to include a screenshot of server logs with errors

What is Message Broadcaster
Moore has the Message broadcaster which passes information to their external Process Server. This external process server expects a specific schema. That schema not being present means it will ignore the extraneous messages. Meaning if the Broadcaster isn’t configured properly the messages won’t be consumed at all and will not throw an error.

Alert: Msg Broadcaster Down or Inactive
If you receive the alert below indicating that the message broadcaster is down, and you find that the Message Broadcaster is inactive, please follow the steps outlined below to address the issue.

Why Are We Getting These Alerts
Msg Broadcaster alert is configured based on the heartbeat being stale by a minute. Ongoing major system activity has been shown to impact the heartbeat just as much as the broadcaster itself choking on CTM or its own queries. General system slowness has been also reported.

chat.answer:
To address a down Message Broadcaster:

1. Verify the status in Web Admin and open Support mailbox simultaneously. If Not Running, notify MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately (quoted: "If the broadcaster is Active... If the status is Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately").

2. Follow consistent communication by updating every few minutes on the situation.

3. Investigate based on scenarios provided:
   - For CTM Blocking, terminate blocking SPIDs with prod CRDADMIN credentials or escalate to US/Dubai contacts (quoted: "If you find blocking in the Support mailbox... Escalate to the following team members if you do not have prod CRDADMIN credentials").
   - For Non-CTM Blocking, escalate for confirmation before killing commands.
   - For long-running transactions, kill broadcast queries or escalate for approval.
   - If no issues found, review logs and initiate a failover to backup middle tier.

How to Merge a Ticket in HaloITSM

Identify the Tickets you wish to Merge From and Into.For the Tickets you wish to Merge INTO another ticket, checkbox them.Hover your Mouse over the Edit Icon and navigate to the Merge Button, Click that.Once you've selected the merge button you will find this merge ticket(s) screen. Locate the ticket by its ID or Summary Title that you wish to merge the chosen ticket(s) into. (Be sure to adjust the filters as necessary). When you want to Merge a ticket into another one.

chat.answer:
To merge a ticket in HaloITSM, follow these steps:

1. Identify the Tickets you wish to Merge From and Into.
2. Checkbox the tickets you wish to Merge INTO another ticket.
3. Hover your Mouse over the Edit Icon, navigate to the Merge Button, and Click that.
4. On the "Merge ticket(s)" screen, Locate the ticket by its ID or Summary Title that you wish to merge the chosen ticket(s) into. (Be sure to adjust the filters as necessary). 
5. Click on the chosen ticket and then confirm selection.

"The system will close the tickets you merged and leave a note on the ticket they were merged into, including adding all of the history of the original ticket."

question: CR wont let me cancel a fix trade
expected: Cancel FIX Trades in CRD When Blocked

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

How to Cancel FIX Trades in CRD When Cancel Action Is Blocked

CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender, and know the content is safe. Hi The below order has flowed into CR from a bad vcon, please cancel, it won’t let us: Thanks Chris *** Moore Europe Legal Disclaimer and Other Information*** Moore Europe comprises of Moore Europe Capital Management, LLP and MECM, Limited; these are investment management firms, who buy and sell equities and other financial instruments. Please do not provide us any inside information or material nonpublic information. We may buy or sell financial instruments based on the information you provide. We intend our securities and futures trading to remain unrestricted, and we do not agree to restrict our activities in any way. Please do not send us any information you are obliged to keep confidential, are not authorised to disclose to us, or are not authorised to have, whether any such restriction is imposed by law, agreement, government or company policy. This applies to information you obtain from any source. We are under no obligation to keep any information we receive from you confidential unless we sign a confidentiality agreement in advance. We ask that you provide to us only information that fully complies with these requirements and restrictions and that does not constitute inside information or material nonpublic information. . Moore Europe Capital Management, LLP - One Curzon Street, London, W1J 5HA, is registered as a limited liability partnership in England and Wales (No: OC322533) with its registered office at 27 Old Gloucester Street, London, United Kingdom, WC1N 3AX and is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority. MECM, Limited - One Curzon Street, London, W1J 5HA, is registered as a private limited company in England and Wales (No: 03144922) with its registered office at 27 Old Gloucester Street, London, United Kingdom, WC1N 3AX. Disclaimer Link: To see Legal Disclaimer and other important information, click the link below or copy and paste the link into your browser's address line. https://d.moorecap.com/ukdisclaimer.html

CRD will not allow a cancellation if there are active or pending FIX messages associated with the order. 
In these scenarios, the cancellation must be processed using FIX Override.


CRD also requires that the Exec Broker on the placement level matches the original broker used for the fill (e.g., BBGFIT for VCON orders).
If the Exec Broker is incorrect (for example, set to GSIL instead of BBGFIT), CRD will disable the FIX Override button, preventing the user from cancelling the order.



Please follow the steps below
1. Check the Exec Broker StatusBefore proceeding:
If the Exec Broker on the placement level is correct → You may proceed directly with FIX Override (Step 3).
If the Exec Broker on the placement level is incorrect or empty → CRD disables FIX Override. The Exec Broker on the placement level must be corrected to the original broker the order was filled with before the cancellation can be performed (Step 2).
2. Re-enable the FIX Override ButtonIf the Override buttons are greyed out, Update the Exec Broker at the placement level:
Open the order.
Locate the OP__EXEC_BROKER
Set the Exec Broker to the original broker associated with the fill.
Save

For example, for VCON-originated orders, set Exec Broker to BBGFIT
Updating this value typically re-enables the FIX Override button.


3. Apply FIX OverrideOnce the button is active:
Click the Override button
Enter your user initials and the click "Proceed with FIX Override"
Proceed with the Cancel action by changing the status on the order to CNCL via dropdown
Save

Clients may request assistance cancelling a trade that has come into CRD from FIX when they are unable to cancel it themselves and get an error prompt (as shown in the example screenshots).

Example request:



Error after cancel attempt by changing from status column (set to "cncl" status) from the blotter and not the Custom Button "MCM Cancel Acctg":

chat.answer:
CRD will not allow you to cancel a FIX trade if there are active or pending FIX messages associated with it. You must use FIX Override to cancel such orders.

Quote: "CRD will not allow a cancellation if there are active or pending FIX messages associated with the order. In these scenarios, the cancellation must be processed using FIX Override."

Additionally, ensure that the Exec Broker on the placement level matches the original broker used for the fill (e.g., BBGFIT for VCON orders). If it does not match, update the Exec Broker to re-enable the FIX Override button.

Follow Steps 2 and 3 from the document to correct the Exec Broker if necessary and apply the FIX Override.

question: the us1 proc server thing for recon

ask.answer:
Source: Obsidian Notes

4.1 Checking the Tidal Recon Job

# Summary Action

Category: Moore

Tidal Jobs errors are observed. Next step is to check the files on the us1-proc02 machine here: [[7. us1-proc02 Login]]

Refer to the below for details on this step.

# Detailed Action

The TIDAL Jobs responsible for pulling files are:

| **Prime Broker** | **TIDAL Job Name** |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| CITI | 010_W_RECON_CITI_PB_PULL |
| Goldman (different files use different Tidal jobs, batch and Script files) | 020_W_RECON_GOLDMAN_PB_PULL 019_W_RECON_GOLDMAN_PRIO_PULL |
| JPM | 090_W_RECON_JPM_PB_PULL |
| Morgan | 070_W_RECON_MORGAN_PB_PULL |
| Chase | 050_W_RECON_CHASE_PB_PULL |
| BOA | 005_W_RECON_BOA_PB_PULL |

The tidal jobs that pull the files are the first thing to check. They are all under the Group “RECON_DAILY_BATCH > PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL” .

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tidal jobs.png]

In Tidal, we first select the PB retrieval job that is suspect from the email alert.

1. Note the job Status: Check if job has completed normally, abnormally, or not run at all.

1. Note that an abnormal completion indicates an error that needs to be investigated

2. Normal completion does not rule out file issues. If a job completes normally, investigation into missing / bad date files is still needed as per the alert email.

2. Right-click on the job, select “Details” and select the “Output” tab. This tab will show what the job has done. Example outputs: (in the below Run 3 is the latest, Run 2 is the previous and Run 1 is the first.

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tidal Job output downloaded files.png]

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tidal Job output tab content.png]

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tidal job Output tab content1.png]

3. 1. Not all errors can be accurate so Tidal job output errors need to also be confirmed by checking actual events on the US2-PROC2 server. This is explained in the [[4.2 Checking Files on us1-proc02]] Section.

Related notes:
- 7. us1-proc02 Login
- Recon Tidal jobs.png
- Recon Tidal Job output downloaded files.png
- Recon Tidal Job output tab content.png
- Recon Tidal job Output tab content1.png

chat.answer:
To check the US1 proc server for recon issues, follow these steps:

1. "The tidal jobs that pull the files are the first thing to check. They are all under the Group ‘RECON_DAILY_BATCH > PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL’."
2. Select the suspect job from the email alert and note its status: "Check if job has completed normally, abnormally, or not run at all."
3. If a job completes normally but issues persist, investigate missing/bad date files as per the alert email.
4. For abnormal completions, right-click on the job, select “Details,” then check the “Output” tab for what the job has done.

For further investigation of errors, "Tidal job output errors need to also be confirmed by checking actual events on the US2-PROC2 server." Refer to [[4.2 Checking Files on us1-proc02]] Section for details.

question: that lock file error on recon jobs

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

2.5 TIDAL Recon - Bad Dates - Clearing Lock Files

Steps to clear the lock file:

Open PasswordState, and navigate to the Recon folder found in the menu on the left. Log into us1-proc02, as NYCProc. Open K Recon folder, shortcut found in desktop, go to FTP → Clear Lockfiles, and run the ClearLockfile.bat of the Prime Broker we are facing the issue for:
Open image-20220627-101130.pngIn case there was no ClearLockfile.bat of the prime broker we are facing the issue for, follow the below steps:
Open the PB_PULL job to see what .bat file is it running. Ex: we had the job 010_W_RECON_CITI_PB_PULL and it was running CitiCopy.bat. 
Open image-20220627-082354.pngGo to us1-proc02 server, open K Recon shortcut folder found in desktop, go to FTP, right click on the .bat file, and select edit. 
Open image-20220627-094850.pngSelect the first plink statement and stop at dropoff (as highlighted below) then copy it:
Open image-20220627-095106.pngOpen command window, paste the copied plink statement, and hit enter:
Open image-20220627-095351.pngType in the below commands:
bash
cd workdir
rm -rf *
ls (to check if the workdir directory is empty, it should be empty)
Rerun PB_PULL Tidal job.

Sometimes, when rerunning the failed PB_PULL job, the job may fail due to the below error, shown in tidal job output:

Open image-20220627-080841.pngA lock file is generated in the download directory of the Prime Broker upon running the PB_PULL job for the first time. When rerunning the PB_PULL job for the second time (or more), the lockfile will block the download process. In this case, we have to clear the lockfile and rerun the job again.

chat.answer:
To resolve the lock file error on recon jobs:

"Log in to us1-proc02 as NYCProc, open K Recon folder and go to FTP → Clear Lockfiles, then run the ClearLockfile.bat for the specific Prime Broker."

If there is no ClearLockfile.bat available:
- Open the PB_PULL job to identify the .bat file it runs.
- Edit that .bat file in the K Recon shortcut folder under FTP.
- Copy the first plink statement up to dropoff.
- Paste this into a command window and execute.
- In the command window, type: **bashcd workdir**, then **rm -rf * **, followed by **ls** to confirm the directory is empty.

Rerun the PB_PULL Tidal job after these steps.

1. 1. The name of the script (.sh) file that contains the steps to pull files from sFTP site (./bin/GetDcCh.sh in the example above)

2. Copy the part of the line from the beginning until just after the word dropoff. All quotes before the word dropoff need to included, but not any quotes after the word dropoff.

1. Some batch files have a path before the plink, and that should be copied as well.

[Embedded image OCR from: Pasted image 20251229204300.png]

5. paste the copied plink from step 4b. into a CMD window prompt:

[Embedded image OCR from: example .sh file and copy.png]

[Embedded image OCR from: Pasted image 20251229204335.png]

6. Find the FTP command path and the login credentials. The net quick steps are:

1. Type "bash” then hit enter, then (sometimes the cmd opens in bash mode)

2. Type “cd bin” > that takes us into the bin directory where the .sh files are found

3. Type both or the second item from the 2 points below:

1. ls > lists the files in the current directory (bin in our case) to confirm the .sh file required os in the current directory (can be skipped for familiar users), or

2. ```cat filename.sh``` (in our example, ```cat GetDcCh.sh```) > this will

1. Relevant .sh file is listed in the [[3. RECON File Mappings|Recon File Mapping]] Sheet in Column I.

2. list the content of the .sh file that contains the the FTP command location and

3. the login credentials for the PB FTP site in question.

4. It also shows the path inside the FTP where the files will be dropped by the PB and is usually mentioned just after the ```username@domain>:/ ``` part (eg. inbound, incoming, or direct on the FTP without any subfolder like the case is in our example from Citi.

[Embedded image OCR from: Pasted image 20251229205723.png]

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon sFTP login creds from sh file.png]

7. From current cmd line, enter the path of the sftp command followed by the value observed in the file. The cursor should change from “bash $:” to sftp> along with a message confirming connection established

[Embedded image OCR from: Pasted image 20251230004645.png]

8. 1. Navigate to the folder where the PBs will drop the file. Not required for the example above from Citi, but for GS for example, the folder is outgoing: sftp> cd outgoing, followed by enter.

9. Searching for the file with bad dates for PBs occurs here using the ls command. Type “ls -ltr filename” followed by enter, or “ls -ltr "first few characters from filename”" followed by enter, and this will show us matching files, size and time they were added on the sftp server.

1. Time value can be used to determine if the file was on the sftp server before the pull job was executed in Tidal.

1. for example of the file timestamp of the file in question is 6:35 and the Tidal job run timestamps is 6:40, this most likely suggests that the file should have been pulled when the Tidal job ran.

2. If the Tidal job runtime timestamp is before the file timestamp on sftp, this likely means that the pull job at runtime did not pull the file in question.

10. Action following investigation:

1. If File was on sftp before the runtime of the Tidal PB Pull job, then we need to confirm why the file was not pulled after rechecking the files in the PB and incoming folders do not reflect the file in the sftp server.

2. If the file was on sftp after the runtime of the Tidal PB Pull job, then we need to take the following actions:

1. Rerun the Tidal PB Pull job that is responsible for pulling the files in question. If this fails, then

2. Manually download the file from sftp and move it to the appropriate folders for the Recon.

question: recon file missing, what do I do

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

2.1 - TIDAL Recon - Bad Dates - Step 1: Tidal Job Checks (DRAFT)

Check the job: PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL (Under RECON_DAILY_BATCH group) in Tidal.Select the job of the targeted broker, the one mentioned in the alert (in ticket).Note job status.Check job output to check if files were downloaded and look for failure messages. If job failed and output shows failure is due to lock file (error shown below), clear the lock files as per the instructions in this page..List of files, as shown below, is present in output, a 100% is shown next to each file name to indicate it was downloaded. If it's not a 100%, it wasn't downloaded. Double check, if it wasn't downloaded, using steps in 3.2 to make sure files aren't present. Check for the files mentioned initially in the ticket. If job is not running or waiting on dependencies, attempt to rerun it, if possible as sometimes rerunning could be greyed out. (Say no to running dependencies). If this doesn't work, proceed to steps in 3.2.If job completed normally and output shows files were downloaded properly, as per step 6, proceed to steps in 3.2.

The reconciliation jobs that we frequently encounter issues with are defined in Tidal under the group name "RECON_DAILY_BATCH". Each job is responsible for pulling specific files from a designated prime broker, and the name of the prime broker is included in the job name for easy identification.



The process for checking these jobs and taking action is the same each time, however, depending on which primer broker is mentioned in the alert, the steps should be performed for the job associated with that specific Prime Broker. 

The following steps and checks should be carried out whenever any of the previously mentioned errors—bad dates or missing file alerts—are encountered in the incoming ticket.

The following page provides an introduction about the expected alerts for Recon - Bad Dates ticket:
Recon - Bad Dates - Intro

Expected Scenarios:
Job failed with lock file output.Job completed normally and output shows files were downloaded.Job is stuck on waiting on dependencies or didn't run.Job output shows files were not downloaded.

chat.answer:
If your recon file is missing, first check the relevant TIDAL job status under "RECON_DAILY_BATCH > PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL" group. Note the job Status: "Check if job has completed normally, abnormally, or not run at all." (Source: Obsidian Notes)

For a normal completion, investigate missing/bad date files as per the alert email. For an abnormal completion, investigate the error indicated in the Tidal job output details on the "Output" tab. Additionally, confirm any errors by checking actual events on the US2-PROC2 server as detailed in [[4.2 Checking Files on us1-proc02]].

question: how to manually load a file in the recon tool
expected: 5. Manual File Loading in RECON Tool

ask.answer:
Source: Obsidian Notes

5. Manual File Loading in RECON Tool

Category: Moore

Once the correct files are in the PBs folders, we may be requested by Patrick to manually load the files into the RECON Tool.
To open the RECON tool, simply click the icon on the windows bar

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tool app icon.png]

When we first open the RECON Tool, just click >>LOGIN. Credentials are automatically filled. This is the opening page of the RECON Tool:

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Tool UI screenshot.png]

The Two Sections below:

- Sanity Check Before Loading Files, and

- Sanity Check Completed - Loading of Files

Have to be performed for every file that is required to be loaded.

# Sanity Check Before Loading Files

Before any loading takes place, a due diligence that none of the missing / bad date files have already been loaded is required.

1. Navigate to Tasks > Undo Read. Wait a few seconds and another window will open

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon navigating Undo read.png]

1. Check required to ensure the files to be loaded are not already loaded

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Undo Read search fields.png]

2. We will fill in 3 fields in the opened Window (Undo Read) based on the mappings found columns F, G and H in the [[3. RECON File Mappings |Recon File Mappingd]] Sheet that are available per file:

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon File format and batch job name.png]

3. 1. Fill in the three fields in the following order:

1. Batch Job > from Column H

2. Data Source > from Column F

3. File Format > from Column G

4. Check the results:

1. Find the file we are planning to load

2. Confirm that column “Read In” does not include current data

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon checking for loaded data.png]

3. 1. We can see that latest read in file is 12/22/2025 in our example. so if the file needs to be loaded for 12/23/2025, then we are ok to move to load.

1. 1. Close the Undo Read window by clicking the red X on the top right of the window or clicking the “Close” button on the lower right side of the window.

2. Navigate to Tasks > Data Load

[Embedded image OCR from: RECON navigating for data load.png]

3. three sections of that Data Load window need to be modified:

[Embedded image OCR from: Recon Data Load Screen.png]

1. 1. File Load Type will remain as “File”

2. Data Source will be set to the value in column F for the required file (Transaction, Cash or Holding)

3. File Format will be set as per the value in Column G, in our case “GS Cash Activity”

4. Select the required file from the three dots option in the File Name(s) section.

4. Click Load and monitor the output in the bottom section of the window “Data Load Progress Status”. there will be skipped rows, and then loaded rows.

5. If there are no errors and we can see loaded and skipped rows, then once all required files are loaded, update Patrick that File is loaded and if he can confirm and state if there are any additional requirements for us to check.

6. If the file shows it is Read In on current date we want to load the file for, then confirm with Patrick that we see the same file loaded in RECON tool having the current Read In date.

1. Stop here. Do not load. Wait for Confirmation from Patrick on required actions. Escalate as necessary to ensure proper resolution.

question: weekend restart procedure for moore prod

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

Automated 21R2 Weekend Restart

Errors Encountered If you see that the Tidal Job did not run try to rerun the job and if that doesn't work try to use the scripts to manually restart the services. You can find the steps in this page: https://agent.omnivista.com/kb?id=80 If Tidal is down, notify helpdesk@moorecap.com , helpdesk-london@moorecap.co.uk . You can proceed with checks and restart servers manually instead.

Job Did Not Run
If the received emails indicate that the job group was successful, please proceed to 'Server Checks'. However, if the job group completed abnormally, please check Tidal to see if the jobs ran and if they ran and completed normally proceed with checks. If it completed abnormally, try to rerun the job and see if it completed normally, if not, please call Zahraa Awad.
Tidal Job is CRD_WEEKLY_RESTART



Server Checks
1. Log in to your assigned Moore machine:
Go to NetScaler AAA 



b. Log in using your Moore username (ex. omni_zawad) and password.

2. Access each of UAT, QA, and PROD CRD Web Admin to check that the services are running:
PROD:
Using Google Chrome, go to us1-crd02:8081/crts/ 
Log in with your individual user credentials
You should see 18 active CharlesRiver services:
us1-crd028081
us1-crd038081
us1-crd038082
us1-crd048081
us1-crd048082
us1-crd058081
us1-crd058082
us1-crd068081
us1-crd068082
us1-crd078081
us1-crd088081
us1-crd088082
us1-crd108081
us1-crd118081
us1-crd118082
us1-crd128081
us1-crd128082
us1-crdfix018081



b. On us1-crd02:8081/crts/ Webadmin, we need to check the below:
RTAPI status should be 1/1 running for all services except us1-crd108081, us1-crd118081, us1-crd118082, and us1-crdfix018081.
If RTAPI status on one of the services is 0/1 running, go to that service's RTAPI on Webadmin, Stop it then start it and click save.
Message Broadcaster should have status Active for us1-crd078081. (Note - if you see it Active in us1-crd088081 that means that was due to a recent failover, okay to proceed.) 
Message Processors should have status 1 running for us1-crd078081. (Note - if you see it Active in us1-crd088081 that means that was due to a recent failover, okay to proceed.) 
Trade Gateway should be 3 configured for all middle tiers.
CTM is Active.
Go to Trade Gateway on us1-crd118082.
Go to CTM.
It should have Connection Status and Status Request as Active and Auto-Start Status Requestor is set to Yes.

If they are not in active status, stop Status Requestor → Unlock Credentials → Start Status Requestor.If step iv didn’t work, reach out to ops@moorecap.com, cc Maher (maher.almanakly@moorecap.com) and inform them.Workflow Rules should have status Active for us1-crd108081.Ensure the "Table Event Publisher" is active on US1-CRD118081. If it is not Active, refer to this PageRandomly check 4 to 5 of the MTs' "cr_server.log" files once the previous checks are completed, and make sure we do not see Errors in the log that are repeating regularly after the section "Java started with" from the log file. The errors could be multiple errors, so it is not currently possible to list all, but the log should look similar to the below, where no errors are observed and confirmation on the "Artemis" service is connected (check red rectangle).Search he log for "DistributedLock", and make sure the client and server both point to prod MTs and that there are no UAT servers mentioned:If we do see errors after the restart kick in, then likely a manual restart of all the MTs is required. Refer to this document.
MOVE TO NEXT SECTION

3. UAT:
Using Google Chrome, go to us2uat-cr03:8081/crts/ 
Log in with your individual user credentials
You should see 6 active CharlesRiver services:
us2uat-cr038081
us2uat-cr048081
us2uat-cr048082
us2uat-cr058081
us2uat-cr058082
us2uat-cr068081



4. QA:
Using Google Chrome, go to us2qa-cr01:8081/crts/ 
Log in with your individual user credentials
You should see 2 active Charles River services:
us2qa-cr018081
us2qa-cr028081



Sending Out Email
 If you have access to Halo, there will be a ticket for the Weekend Restart, make sure to Triage, Take Ownership and do the needed steps for the ticket.
If you do not have access to Halo, send an email to Maher (maher.almanakly@moorecap.com), Andrew (andrew.adams@omnivista.com), charlesriversupport@moorecap.com and cc omv-moore-support@omnivistasolutions.com to inform that the restart was completed successfully.
Subject: Weekly CRD Restart Checks - Sunday July 2nd, 2023
Body:
Hello Maher and Andrew,
Please note that the weekly restart completed normally. All CRD services are running and triggers and audit parameters are enabled.

Every Sunday, we initiate a restart of the Moore CRD servers for three environments: QA, UAT, and PROD. This process occurs at 12:30 am EST, which corresponds to 8:30 AM Dubai time, facilitated by Tidal jobs. The jobs are expected to conclude by 12:50 am EST, and subsequently, we anticipate receiving an email confirming the successful completion of the jobs. (Please note the time changes during daylight savings)
To ensure that the jobs have been completed successfully and that all the necessary services are active and running, we need to conduct several checks before 3 am EST which is 11 am Dubai time.
To execute the weekend restart checks, follow the below steps:
Check that we receive the success/failure emails for the weekly restart job group. These emails should be sent to charlesriversupport@moorecap.com. We will be receiving three emails, one for each environment (QA/UAT, PROD). Example emails are seen in the screenshots below.
QA/UAT: The email is sent from SchedulerQA@moorecap.com.
PROD: The email is sent from Scheduler@moorecap.com.

   "Every Sunday, we initiate a restart of the Moore CRD servers... and subsequently, we anticipate receiving an email confirming the successful completion of the jobs."

2. If the job group completed abnormally, try to rerun the job. If that doesn't work, call Zahraa Awad.

3. Proceed with server checks by logging into your assigned Moore machine via NetScaler AAA using your credentials.
   
   "Log in to your assigned Moore machine: Go to NetScaler AAA"

4. Access PROD CRD Web Admin at us1-crd02:8081/crts/ and check that the 18 active CharlesRiver services are running as specified.

5. Ensure RTAPI status, Message Broadcaster, Message Processors, Trade Gateway, CTM, Workflow Rules, and "Table Event Publisher" meet the stated criteria for PROD environment.

6. After completing checks, send an email to Maher (maher.almanakly@moorecap.com), Andrew (andrew.adams@omnivista.com), charlesriversupport@moorecap.com and cc omv-moore-support@omnivistasolutions.com confirming the restart was completed successfully.

question: how do I copy FIX logs to my machine
expected: How to copy FIX Logs to local machine

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

How to copy FIX Logs to local machine

FIX Log LocationsWhen logged into a Soros virtual workstation, open File Explorer to access the FIX message logs at the below locations:
Prod File Location: \\vsompcrv05\logs\cameron_buy.log
Archived Prod Logs: \\nyc-sor-vlog-01\Logs\CharlesRiver\ProdV17\FIX
UAT File Location: \\vrchdcrv01\CharlesRiver\17_mt1\serverapps\logs
Soros moves FIX logs that are more than 3 days old into an Archive folder. If you don’t see the logs for the date that you need in the Prod file location, they were likely moved to the Archive folder.

Accessing FIX logviewer.batNavigate to the following location inside of the UAT Middle tier:
C:\CharlesRiver\17_mt1\serverapps\fix\bin\logviewer.bat

Copying the FIX LogsWhenever you are viewing FIX logs, it is important to copy them to a local location first before viewing. Otherwise, if you open them directly, the FIX Engine will no longer be able to write to them, which can cause catastrophic breaks.

There two ways we can copy the FIX logs to a safe, location:
Via Command Prompt
Via File Explorer

Copying Via File ExplorerOpen File Explorer
Type in the FIX Log Location that you need.
Then copy the cameron_buy.log file and paste it to your virtual workstation in a location of your preference. For example, you may paste it directly to your Desktop for quick access and then move it to a more organized subfolder later. 
Copying Via Command PromptThis method has a few extra steps due to the fact that we can’t access our user working folders from the command prompt (this is because they are on a separate network drive). However, this method is safer than the above since you don’t run the risk of mis-clicking when attempting to copy the file.
From the Start Menu on your Soros virtual workstation, launch Command Prompt
Identify your preferred file location.
In the example below, the preferred file location is the 'Documents' folder, i.e., C:\User\ \Documents
Execute the following command: copy \\ /Y 
In this example, the command is 'copy \\vsompcrv05\logs\cameron_buy.log /Y C:\Users\torrek\Documents'
The target environment is Production, so we access 'vsompcrv05'. 
We want the latest FIX logs, so we target 'cameron_buy.log'. Prior days' logs will be appended with the date (e.g. 'cameron_buy.log.06-25-2025'). 
Kathleen Torres is the user in this example, so she is using her own Documents folder for the destination folder. 
The '/Y' flag suppresses prompts that would confirm whether you want to overwrite existing files in the destination folder.
You can learn more about the 'copy' command by searching online for 'Windows Commands copy'.

To make the logs accessible for viewing in the UAT middle tier, you will need to then copy and paste this file into your User Working folder.
Open image-202501-220737.pn

chat.answer:
To copy FIX logs to your machine:

Via File Explorer:
"Type in the FIX Log Location that you need. Then copy the cameron_buy.log file and paste it to your virtual workstation in a location of your preference."

Via Command Prompt:
"From the Start Menu on your Soros virtual workstation, launch Command Prompt. Identify your preferred file location. Execute the following command: 'copy \\vsompcrv05\logs\cameron_buy.log /Y C:\Users\torrek\Documents'." 

Ensure you replace "C:\Users\torrek\Documents" with your actual Documents folder path.

question: steps for one madison data load failure
expected: One Madison Data Load Status FAILURE article

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

One Madison Daily File Load Failure – Error Format Correction & Reload to Snowflake

Access the File in AWS S3:
Navigate to the daily-files-to-snowflake/load folder.
Locate the file, select it, and download it to your local machine.
Open the File:
When prompted, click ‘Don’t Convert’ to maintain original formatting.
Identify the Issue:
Based on the error message, locate the column that is erroring.
Apply filters to find the problematic row(s).
Correct the Format:
Open the file erroring using Notepad++, and identify where the issue is, if it's a field that is causing the error, fix it, in this case it was a trailing empty Row at the end of the excel file, we removed it and uploaded the file, as shown below row 1549 is the empty row that we removed.
Save the Corrected File:
Save the file with a new version name by adding V2 before the date.
Example:JSC-Position_as_on_2025.02.21.csv ➔ JSC-Position_as_on_V2_2025.02.21.csv
Replace the File in AWS S3:
Delete the original file from S3 (confirm deletion by typing ‘delete’).
Upload the corrected V2 file to the same location.
Load the File into Snowflake:
Trigger the file load process.
Ensure you receive the message: ‘Files Loaded Successfully’.
Verify the Data Load:
Run the provided SELECT statement in Snowflake should show the same date as the file date which usually is the previous day.
SELECT MAX(TRADEDATE) FROM DBO.VITEOS_POSITIONS

Communicate Completion:
Once verified, send a confirmation email to Scott indicating successful resolution.



Other issues faced and their resolutions:
Issue: Invalid UTF8 detected in string.

Excel file showing a weird Dash – instead of -


Resolution:
Replace all weird – with - using find and replace all in excel, and then follow the same process of deleting the file from the bucket, renaming the correct file with V2 and then upload it to the bucket and run the related snowflake job.

Each day around 7 AM EST (3 PM Dubai Time), we receive automated emails via Halo regarding the One Madison file load status. If the status indicates:
“Overall Status: Successful, no additional action required”, no further steps are needed.
However, if a failure ticket is generated, typically due to incorrect data formats (e.g., date vs. timestamp), immediate action is required to correct the file and reload it into Snowflake. This ensures proper data processing and system continuity.

chat.answer:
Steps for correcting a One Madison data load failure:

1. "Navigate to the daily-files-to-snowflake/load folder. Locate the file, select it, and download it to your local machine."
2. Identify the issue by locating the column causing errors based on the error message.
3. Correct the format: "Open the file erroring using Notepad++, and identify where the issue is... in this case it was a trailing empty Row at the end of the excel file, we removed it..."
4. Save the corrected file with a new version name (e.g., add V2 before the date).
5. Replace the file in AWS S3: "Delete the original file from S3 (confirm deletion by typing ‘delete’). Upload the corrected V2 file to the same location."
6. Load the file into Snowflake and verify the data load with the provided SELECT statement.
7. Communicate completion by sending a confirmation email to Scott indicating successful resolution.

question: broadcaster is down, who do I contact and what do I check

ask.answer:
Source: Kb Docs

Message Broadcaster Down

Step 1: Initial Status Check
Verify the status of the Message Broadcaster in Web Admin and open the Support mailbox simultaneously.
If the broadcaster is Active on us1-crd078081 (primary) or us1-crd088081 (backup), notify the MCM CRIMS Support – External chat that the alert has been received and the broadcaster is currently active. Also note that you will continue to monitor it's status. 
If the status is Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately and proceed to investigate further.
Message Broadcaster not running screenshot here

Step 2: Consistent Communication
Maintain regular communication with the external Moore chat, providing updates every few minutes if the broadcaster remains down, if the root cause is still being investigated, or what actions are being taken

Scenarios for Investigation:

Scenario 1: CTM Blocking
If you find blocking in the Support mailbox, identify whether the SPIDs with a Blocker value of 0 are CTM related or not (captured below). If they are proceed with the following:
Access the prod database using the CRDADMIN login and terminate the blocking SPIDs.
Escalate to the following team members if you do not have prod CRDADMIN credentials:
US: Andrew, Anuja
Dubai: Raed, Mike, Mark (Moore)


Scenario 2: Non-CTM Blocking
If the command is not a CTM command, escalate to Andrew and/or Moore contacts (Maher or Prasad) for confirmation on what the process is before killing it.
Commands that should not be killed without explicit approval include (but are not limited to):
TRIG_TS_ORDER_FIRM_POSITION
TS_ORDER_AU
Fn_Append_Allocation_Fields
Broadcast Queries
Add new examples here


Scenario 3: No Blocking but Long-Running Transactions
Navigate to Web Admin > Monitor and the Support mailbox to identify any existing long-running transactions.
If the transaction is broadcast query related, it should be killed.
Broadcast queries can be killed in the case of long-running transactions vs. a blocking because as a long running transaction, the process has not committed yet.
If unrelated, escalate to Andrew, Maher, or Prasad to get confirmation if the process can be terminated.


Scenario 4: No Blocking, No Long-Running Transactions
Review the Message Logs and Server Logs for any errors.
If errors are found, resolve appropriately (examples include restarting services, reboot middle tier, or adjust broadcast queries with Maher).
If no errors are found, initiate a failover to the backup middle tier.
Include steps here
Need to include a screenshot of server logs with errors

What is Message Broadcaster
Moore has the Message broadcaster which passes information to their external Process Server. This external process server expects a specific schema. That schema not being present means it will ignore the extraneous messages. Meaning if the Broadcaster isn’t configured properly the messages won’t be consumed at all and will not throw an error.

Alert: Msg Broadcaster Down or Inactive
If you receive the alert below indicating that the message broadcaster is down, and you find that the Message Broadcaster is inactive, please follow the steps outlined below to address the issue.

Why Are We Getting These Alerts
Msg Broadcaster alert is configured based on the heartbeat being stale by a minute. Ongoing major system activity has been shown to impact the heartbeat just as much as the broadcaster itself choking on CTM or its own queries. General system slowness has been also reported.

chat.answer:
**Initial Action:** Verify the status of the Message Broadcaster in Web Admin and open the Support mailbox simultaneously. If it's Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately.  
*"If the broadcaster is Active on us1-crd078081 (primary) or us1-crd088081 (backup), notify the MCM CRIMS Support – External chat that the alert has been received and the broadcaster is currently active."*  
*"If the status is Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately..."*

**Next Steps:** Follow the provided scenarios for investigation, starting with checking for blocking in the Support mailbox.

- `1. Confirm duplicate records in SnowflakeLog in to Snowflake.
Identify the table mentioned in the client’s ticket.
Run a select query to confirm that duplicate records exist. 
Capture evidence (screenshot or query output) if needed for internal notes.
2. Request the corrected file from the vendorAfter confirming duplicates exist in the table, request the corrected file from the vendor.
Send an email to the vendor using the below addresses:JSCNAV@intertrustgroup.comKunal.Kukreja@cscglobal.com
In your email, mention the file name asking for the correct file with no duplicate records.
3. Delete only the affected records from the Snowflake tableImportant: Only delete records related to the affected date / file. Do not run a blanket delete without a proper filter, if in doubt ask someone experienced.Once you have the corrected file (or confirmation that it’s ready to reload), log in to Snowflake.
Delete only the duplicate records, you can identify the records by specifying the records within a time range.
Verify the number of deleted records matches what you expect (for example by checking the number of rows that were duplicated or the number of rows in the old file).

4. Reload the corrected file from S3 and run the Snowflake stored procedureLog in to the S3 bucket used for data loads.
Upload the corrected file received from Intertrust / Kunal to the appropriate load directory.
In Snowflake, run the stored procedure that corresponds to this table to reload data.
5. Inform with the client that all is goodOnce the table has been corrected and validated:
Send an email to the client confirming that the issue has been addressed and asking them to re-check on their side.
Update the Halo ticket.

This article describes how to investigate and resolve duplicate records in a Snowflake table when the issue is caused by duplicate rows in the source file received from the vendor (Intertrust ).

When a client reports duplicate records in a table, the most common cause is that the source file itself contains duplicate rows. The Snowflake load process will insert what is provided, so if the file has dupes, the table will have dupes.`

- `1. Identify the Trades in Question
Open CRD.Search for the specific trades mentioned in the client ticket. 
2. Verify Post-Trade Order Status
Navigate to the Placement section of the trade.Check the Post-Trd Order Status field.If it shows “No matching required”, it indicates:No post-trade routing rules were satisfied to trigger a CTM rule.
VS

 
3. Access Post-Trade Routing Rules
In CRD, go to: Setup → Post-Trade → Post-Trade Routing Rules.
 
4. Filter for CTM Rules
Search for routing rules where Name is like “CTM”.Review the list of rules.Note the Priority column:Rules with lower priority values run first. So Lower Priority Value = Higher PriorityExample: CTM – Alert has higher priority than CTM, so if the first rule is satisfied, it executes and bypasses the others.
 
5. Analyze CTM Rule Conditions
Take CTM – Alert as an example. This rule is triggered if all of the following conditions are met:
Exec Broker belongs to the CTMFI list.Investment Class belongs to the CTMA list.Exchange belongs to the CTM Exchange List.
 
6. Validate Trade Values
For each CTM rule, compare the trade’s field values against the corresponding lists:Exec Broker vs. CTMFI , if it is in the list.Investment Class vs. CTMA , if it is in the list.Exchange vs. CTM Exchange , if it is in the list. 
7. Report Findings to Client
If mismatches in any list are identified (Meaning if the value is not part of the list), inform the client with clear guidance.Example:“The execution broker used for this trade is not included in the CTMFI list. Please review and update the broker setup if CTM transmission is required.”

SFM Sunday Health Checks - Procedure

1. Log into SFMLog in to your SFM account from your assigned machine.
Saved links are machine-specific. Please use the same machine consistently for easier access.
Designated Omni Shared Machines:
vwrkomnivista1
vwrkomnivista2

2. Access PROD WebAdminNavigate to http://csompcrv04:8086/
Verify all 13 middle-tiers (MTs) are visible and running.
Check logs for any issues. 
Middle-Tier Health CheckAll MTs should appear in the console.
CR Middle-tiers:
No errors should be present in the console (except Locate errors—see reference screenshot).
Confirm RTAPI shows 1/1 for all servers.

3. Check the FIX ConsoleEnsure the following:
FIX Engine status is: Available - NOTE: If at first "Available" appears as "No", refresh the page.
FIX Server status is: Started
Early Sunday: It's normal for FIX connections to show as disconnected.
No critical errors should appear on the console.

4. Steps to Check Logs:Navigate to Diagnostics > Logs > Log Files.
Scroll down to Middle Tier Logs.
Open the file: cr_server.html.
Review the log contents for any errors or warnings.
Repeat this process for each Middle Tier (MT) in the PROD environment. Note: To make it easier, it would be best to save each MT in a bookmark.
Logs may differ on the first and last Sunday of each month due to the CR (Change Request) refresh.
We are only concerned with connection-related errors involving Artemis after the shutdown.
Any errors occurring before the time shown in the screenshot below are acceptable and can be disregarded
The following log entries are also considered acceptable and are okay to proceed with:



5. Create a Test Order in Charles RiverLog in to the Charles River Client using Middle-Tier: csompcrv04:8081 (PROD).

FieldValueTickerZVZZTQuantity100AccountQPTEST@ (delete QPTEST_1 if added)BrokerCITITo find the ticker:
Click on the three dots next to Sec Find and hit Enter.
Once all fields are filled:
Navigate to the Audit tab to retrieve the Order ID.
Click Save to submit the order.

6. Process the OrderGo to any blotter tab:
Right-click > Queries > Orders for Order(s) ID
Locate your order.
Compliance CheckRight-click the order > Actions > Check Compliance
Order status should update from Prelim to PreOK.

Send to TradingRight-click the order > Actions > Send to Trading
If a pop-up appears, click OK.

Take OwnershipRight-click the order > Actions > Take Ownership
Status should change to Open.
7. Place/Fill the OrderRight-click > Actions > Place/Fill
In the popup, set Avg Exec Prc = 2
Click Save
Handling Errors and Custodian SettingsIf you receive an error, click Continue.
Check the Custodian field:
If defaulted to FLEX, change it to CITI.
Wait ~30 seconds for allocation to populate.
If it doesn’t auto-populate, manually input CITI on both lines and then click SAVE.
Before changes: 
8. Post-Trade ProcessingRight-click the order > Actions:
Select Prep for Post-Trade
Then click Send to Post-Trade
Finally, click Send to Accounting
Order sent to Accounting:
Verify Trade Export FileAfter 2–10 minutes, check for the trade export file at: \\sorosfunds\sfmfiles\Group\ftp\Enfusion\ToEnfusion\CharlesRiverTradeExport

If path is not accessible, paste it into File Explorer.
Look for file: ListedTrades_ .csv
Ensure today's timestamp and confirm your ZVZZT order is present.
If you don't see a file, please see the following page: SFM Sunday Health Check - ActiveBatch Enfusion Failure checks

10. Check CTM logs in server 078081

11. Complete the TicketAfter all steps are completed:
Click Resolve/Close Ticket
Send out the appropriate summary email (see template email below and make sure to adjust accordingly): 
Hi Everyone,

Weekend Restart health check for 06/15/2025:

Shutdown and restart for the MTs: all MTs are up and running, after the shutdown/restart completedRTAPI on all instances: EnabledFIX Engine: AvailableCharles RiverFixServer1 Status: StartedTest Order in PROD:Order ID : INSET ORDER IDCustodian after Fill: Did not populate automatically, Manually added "CITI" or Populated AutomaticallyTrade Export File to designated folder: Successful Path:(\\sorosfunds\sfmfiles\Group\ftp\Enfusion\ToEnfusion\CharlesRiverTradeExport)Order cancelled successfully.

A ticket in Halo is scheduled to be generated shortly after the Soros weekly restart is done which will signal that the check needs to occur. The restart is scheduled for 1am EST, and should complete by 1:20am EST. The check should proceed by 1:30am EST.`

Usually clients provide us with a screenshot or trade details so we can re-create the positions in CRD. Using the provided screenshot/information, follow these steps:

1) Log into the QA environment in CRD to draft the position for client confirmation.

2) To create a position in CR: Navigate to Setup -> Accounts & Positions -> Positions



3) In the Positions window, click on the "+ New" icon to create a new position



4) Fill in the needed information (ensure Quantity is entered in the BOD field). DO NOT SAVE


5) From the left pane, navigate to the Lot Inventory



6) Click on the "+ New" icon to add a new tax lot row

7) Using the given screenshot from the client, fill in the needed information (Ensure the Trade Date in the tax lot matches the Order Trade Date shown in the screenshot). DO NOT SAVE



8) Take a screenshot of both the Main Position Window and the Lot Inventory Window and store them locally somewhere to confirm with the client.

9) Send an email to the client, pasting both screenshots and asking them to confirm if the position and tax lot are correct before saving (as shown below).



10) Once the client confirms, log in to the Production environment in CRD and recreate the same position and tax lot by repeating steps 2–7.

11) After filling in the new position and tax lot, click Save.



12) Send a follow-up email to the client letting them know the position and tax lot were created.

Clients may request reinstating/re-creating an expired option in CR so they can flatten their positions.
If the client asks to reinstate/create a long, it means we need to add the long position in CR so they can then book a sell long to flatten.
If the client wants to rebook a sell long, we would create a long so they can flatten it.
A similar request is shown below:Hi, 
 
Can you please reinstate this XBI US 08/15/25 C91 option? It expired on Friday but the short needs to be rebooked as a sell long.

 
 
Thanks,
Will`

CRD will not allow a cancellation if there are active or pending FIX messages associated with the order. 
In these scenarios, the cancellation must be processed using FIX Override.


CRD also requires that the Exec Broker on the placement level matches the original broker used for the fill (e.g., BBGFIT for VCON orders).
If the Exec Broker is incorrect (for example, set to GSIL instead of BBGFIT), CRD will disable the FIX Override button, preventing the user from cancelling the order.



Please follow the steps below
1. Check the Exec Broker StatusBefore proceeding:
If the Exec Broker on the placement level is correct → You may proceed directly with FIX Override (Step 3).
If the Exec Broker on the placement level is incorrect or empty → CRD disables FIX Override. The Exec Broker on the placement level must be corrected to the original broker the order was filled with before the cancellation can be performed (Step 2).
2. Re-enable the FIX Override ButtonIf the Override buttons are greyed out, Update the Exec Broker at the placement level:
Open the order.
Locate the OP__EXEC_BROKER
Set the Exec Broker to the original broker associated with the fill.
Save

For example, for VCON-originated orders, set Exec Broker to BBGFIT
Updating this value typically re-enables the FIX Override button.


3. Apply FIX OverrideOnce the button is active:
Click the Override button
Enter your user initials and the click "Proceed with FIX Override"
Proceed with the Cancel action by changing the status on the order to CNCL via dropdown
Save

- `Checklist StepsCheck that Message Broadcaster in WebAdmin is Active and running in tier us1-crd078081 (failover is in us1-crd088081)

Open image-20250324-120033.pngClick on Active, if it says ‘Not Running’ it could be a blimp 
Check The following: 
Status is saying ‘Passive’ (Passive means its doing what its suppose to)
Check the Broadcast Queries status are ‘Active’ 
Open image-20250324-120923.pngCheck that the memory heap is not high
Click on Monitor→ Memory → Heap → msg broadcaster server
Open image-20250324-121200.pngAdditional ChecksCheck the Server Log that the message broadcaster is on for any alerts of it being down:
Diagnostic → Logs → Log Files → msg broadcaster server → then scroll down to Middle Tier Logs → keep scrolling down until you find the server log
Open image-20250324-121733.png 
Open image-20250324-121949.png 


Open image-20250324-122102.pngAdditional NotesIf we observe a lot of activity (see the screenshot below), it could indicate latency, which may trigger the alert. This could be due to passive status performing its tasks, potentially causing delays in the heartbeat due to other system processes. The alert is simply notifying us that the heartbeat may be experiencing issues, and all we need to do is check. Jobs running longer than usual may be unrelated to PROD.
We can also check the support mailbox to monitor for any alerts. Sometimes, the Active status may be a brief blip, but we should verify if there are any blockers. Additionally, queries may lock the database while running, which can contribute to the delay.
Please check the following:
Monitor in WebAdmin: Latency, Service Calls, Data Events, RTAPI Fetch, FIX Incoming, and Long-Running Transactions.
Identify who is running the job: If you click on the service, a pop-up will appear, and Param 01 will show the user running it.
The message broadcaster processes as much as it can, but it may not be as quick as users would prefer.


Open image-20250325-105928.pngResponse we have provided to client (see ticket 21663 Halo):Hi Mark,
Yes, I discussed the alerts with Andrew. The Message Broadcaster alert is triggered when the heartbeat becomes stale for over a minute. Ongoing system activity can impact the heartbeat, just as issues with the broadcaster itself, such as choking on CTM or handling its own queries, can cause delays. Additionally, general system slowness has also been observed as a contributing factor. Whenever we receive an alert, we promptly conduct thorough checks to ensure everything is functioning correctly. While alerts indicate potential issues, if anything significant is identified, we will communicate it immediately and take the necessary actions.
 
Halo Ticket WorkflowAfter verifying that the middle tier of the message broadcaster is marked as 'Active' but the status shows as 'Passive', please take a screenshot and close the ticket. In your reply to the user, mention that the health check has been completed and everything is functioning properly.

What is Message BroadcasterMoore has the Message broadcaster which passes information to their external Process Server. This external process server expects a specific schema. That schema not being present means it will ignore the extraneous messages. Meaning if the Broadcaster isn’t configured properly the messages won’t be consumed at all and will not throw an error.
Alert: Msg Broadcaster Down or InactiveIf you receive the alert below indicating that the message broadcaster is down, please follow the steps outlined below to address the issue. (Note: if we get an alert that say !This is from test environment check UAT)
Why Are We Getting These AlertsMsg Broadcaster alert is configured based on the heartbeat being stale by a minute. Ongoing major system activity has been shown to impact the heartbeat just as much as the broadcaster itself choking on CTM or its own queries. General system slowness has been also reported.
In the event that this is a legitimate alert and a failover is necessary, refer to the instructions on the following page for further guidance. MCM CRD Service Failover Playbook
The following needs to be checked and if we see any issues it needs to be alerted ASAP.
Not only checking that the broadcaster is Active but also checking the Broadcast Queries are all 'Active'

Open image-20250324-115251.png`
- `Check the file name
Confirm the filename ends with .orderExample of correct format: SWPN163747_60054.orderExample of incorrect format: SWPN163747_60054
Rename the file if needed
If the file is missing .order, rename the file and add .order at the end.
Check if you are resending the same order
The system will treat files with the same exact name as duplicates and will not import them.
If you are resending or sending a revised version of the same order, append a version indicator before .orderExample: SWPN163747_60054v2.order, SWPN163747_60054v3.order
If the filename is correct but the file is still not being imported
Review the middle-tier logs to check for any errors or failures.
If major errors are observed in the logs
Restart the middle-tier service to clear the issue and reattempt processing.

An XML file placed in the C:\ORDERIMP directory was not being picked up by the import process (which is consumed by the Process Server in us2uat-cr038081, us2uat-cr048081, and us2uat-cr058081)`
- `Check the job: PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL (Under RECON_DAILY_BATCH group) in Tidal.Select the job of the targeted broker, the one mentioned in the alert (in ticket).Note job status.Check job output to check if files were downloaded and look for failure messages. If job failed and output shows failure is due to lock file (error shown below), clear the lock files as per the instructions in this page..List of files, as shown below, is present in output, a 100% is shown next to each file name to indicate it was downloaded. If it's not a 100%, it wasn't downloaded. Double check, if it wasn't downloaded, using steps in 3.2 to make sure files aren't present. Check for the files mentioned initially in the ticket. If job is not running or waiting on dependencies, attempt to rerun it, if possible as sometimes rerunning could be greyed out. (Say no to running dependencies). If this doesn't work, proceed to steps in 3.2.If job completed normally and output shows files were downloaded properly, as per step 6, proceed to steps in 3.2.

The reconciliation jobs that we frequently encounter issues with are defined in Tidal under the group name "RECON_DAILY_BATCH". Each job is responsible for pulling specific files from a designated prime broker, and the name of the prime broker is included in the job name for easy identification.



The process for checking these jobs and taking action is the same each time, however, depending on which primer broker is mentioned in the alert, the steps should be performed for the job associated with that specific Prime Broker. 

The following steps and checks should be carried out whenever any of the previously mentioned errors—bad dates or missing file alerts—are encountered in the incoming ticket.

The following page provides an introduction about the expected alerts for Recon - Bad Dates ticket:
Recon - Bad Dates - Intro

Expected Scenarios:
Job failed with lock file output.Job completed normally and output shows files were downloaded.Job is stuck on waiting on dependencies or didn't run.Job output shows files were not downloaded.`

- `Confluence page: https://omnivista.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/MCM/pages/3038543873/Process+Server+Import+files+into+CR+Import+XML+securities

Steps to Ensure the Process Server is Running:
Access WebAdmin
Navigate to the WebAdmin interface.
In the "Services" section, locate Process Server.

2. Select the Active Process Server
Click on the link for the active Process Server under the middle tier.
3. Check the Service Status
Verify if the service is in the "Start" state.
If it’s not started, check the "Start" checkbox and click Start to initiate the service.




4. Ensure Service is Running on All Middle Tiers
Confirm that the Process Server is running on all relevant middle tiers, as files may be picked up in one middle tier and processed on another.
5. Verify Folder Creation & Setup
Ensure that the appropriate folder is created on the middle tier.
Instruct the user to place files in this designated folder. This is a "watch" folder, meaning any files dropped into it will be automatically picked up and processed based on the Refresh Interval set in WebAdmin.

Why This is NeededThe CR service facilitates the automatic import of files, such as XML files. Files are placed in a specific location, and the system automatically processes them to create securities and trades.

When to Use ThisActivate this service upon receiving a request. Ensure that you are working in the UAT environment, as enabling this in production (PROD) requires additional approvals.

What to Consider
Workflow Rule Engine: If the XML file is processed through the workflow rule engine, the associated workflow rules will apply.
Process Server: If the file is being processed for import, it goes through the process server. The process server might encounter files it cannot process.

TroubleshootingIf deeper investigation is required, we can enable debug logging to capture additional details. Typically, we rely on server logs, message logs, message log details, and client logs for diagnostics.`
- `"Do you have any background on this message? Any idea what security ID 111425435 is and who sent the trade?"
The FIX ID and the partial raw fix message are usable for locating the message to identify next steps and identifiers.the FIX ID that is blacked out should match tag 17, which is column FIX_ID in the FIX_INCOMING or FIX_INCOMING_HIST table. The Tag 37 id should match FIX_BROKER_ID on the FIX_INCOMING or FIX_INCOMING_HIST table. These values can be used to look up the message in the these tables and identify what order, if any (TM_ORIG_ID) that this is tied to. And you could pull the raw message here, or utilize the Fix Log Viewer and pull the message from the Fix Log to view there. From that you can review for tags tied to the Security identification, such as 22, 48, and 55, or additional ones based on the Broker associated with the fix connection referenced "indicated as source"From here we could identify what this order was related to and who might've completed the trade if they needed it.Identification of what caused the general error. There is difficulty in troubleshooting the reason for Fix Failure and we want to unblock the user while we identify root cause. Guidelines for when it is okay to ask the user to manually book the trade.
In the event that the Fix Message itself is the culprit due to bad identifiers, bad tag values or similar.Advise that the identifiers (or whatever is the cause) is bad and that to proceed it needs to be manually booked.Once User has agreed to manually book, follow process to clear the message from the Fix Resubmit queue.Still must complete Root Cause assessment and report findings of which Tag/Identifier was causing issues and what field that is tied to on the security in the Sec Master.There is a Fix Failure that is affecting a Fixed Income Security, that security Sec Type Code is NOT CB, and the FIx Failure is due to some calculation that minor adjustments are not resolving. Resolution is no longer timely.
If The Fix message failures are tied to the security master itself and we are not able to timely (10 to 20 minutes, 30 mins max) identify the cause and fix it.Advise user that they need to update the Securities Sec type to CB temporarily as it is a simpler configuration.Notify them that this may cause the Accruals on the order to be incorrect and they will likely need to override those values on the order.Assist with any immediate troubleshooting.Create Parent problem ticket to track the investigation.Must compelte Root Cause investigation/assessment and report back findings of what the cause was."Delete the message, I booked manually", "please skip this error message", "Okay, we'll book this manually. Please clear this."

Login to WebAdminNavigate to Fix ConsoleNavigate to Manage of the Fix ServerNavigate to Resubmit Tabclick on the referenced Messages checkbox, which you can locate with the error and identifiers provided.Click "Clear"Reply back advising this message has been cleared.

SFM users will reach out about issues tied to a CHARLES RIVER FIX RESUBMIT alert message. This message could have any number of errors and each may have a different specific resolution. Below is an example, but this is not exhaustive.

The user may ask us for specific actions based on the above message or ones like it. Our objective is to try and assist according to their ask. But there are a few items to be aware of.

The client has an ActiveBatch job which sends these alerts, originating from the Charles River FIX Resubmit queue, on a regular interval to the Users. They in-turn send these to us for us to take action on based on their directions.

The below instructions are not exhaustive and should be considered as a starting point to assist them if it meets these common asks.`

- `EOD Monitor - CR EOD OKAY / CR EOD ERROREvery day, an email fires indicating the status of EOD. Soros has indicated that EOD failure is highly uncommon. However, when an error does occur, our responsibilities are as follows:
Connect to Active Batch in Production
Navigate to the EOD jobs in /OMS PROD V17/CR EOD/
Attempt to identify the failed job, and the reason for the failure.
Regardless of what you find, within 10 minutes, escalate to Nish.Parit@soros.com and Kinson.Au@soros.com, our colleagues in the ExecutionTech team.
Active Batch EOD JobsThere are a bunch of EOD jobs, tailored to a specific region. SFM defines a region based on a group of exchanges.
If jobs don't have a suffix of DATEROLL or SWEEP, then those jobs combine both these functions. Each of these jobs target a specific exchange list, e.g., AMER is Americas EOD and the exchange list is RGN_AMER.
All of these exchange list starting with RGN_ are used in the EOD jobs.
A good amount of pre-processing procs run before EOD and are stored in /OMS PROD V17/CR EOD/ in Active Batch.


Soros preps for positions and tax lots for next day at EOD. These are SQL stored procs and can be found at /OMS PRODD V17/PositionReconciliation:

Delete Tax Lot designations
For positions, they do the following things:
Delete 0 positions
Delete Expired options
collapse Tax Lots for same custodian and cash/swap indicator
collapse boxed positions.
If a position is long and short for the same custodian, they net them out provided they share same:
security
custodian
account
cash/swap indicator`
- `Error Case 1: MCM Done Failed - Unable to connect to the remote server
When attempting to click MCM Done button, an error message appears indicating that the action has failed for the order due to the following error: Unable to connect to the remote server.
Steps:
1- Access Web Admin through --> Charles River> Sys Admin> Web Amin.

Verify that all Prod Services are up and running.
us1-cr028081 (master), CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr038081, us1-cr038082, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr048081, us1-cr048082, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr058081, us1-cr058082, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr068081, us1-cr068082, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr078081, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr088081, us1-cr088082, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr108081, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-cr118081, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)
us1-crfix018081, CcmExec (SMS Agent Service)

2- If you notice that one or more services are not running, you should start them.
Remote Desktop Connection → Log in to the desired server that is not running.
Ex: If 06:8081 is missing from the Web Admin page, you will need log in to the server us1-cr06.
Go to services window and run the desired service. 
If the service stops after running, attempt first to stop SMS AGENT Service → run the desired service (Ex: CharlesRiverUS1CD068081) → After you verify that the service is now running and check its up in Web Admin → re-run SMS AGENT Service.


Error Case 2: Issuer Assigned to Counterparty does not match the Issuer Assigned to the Security
 
When attempting to use MCM Send to Accting button leads to an error. 
Issuer assigned to counterparty does not match the issuer assigned to the security.


Steps:

1- From Setup → Security → Navigate to the order's security to check the Issuer associated with it. 
(In the example we're using, the Issuer was BARC)

2- Check the Broker assigned with the order facing the error 
(Ex: It was JPM)

3- Check if there is a Counterparty created for the broker by following the steps below:
a- In CR, navigate to Setup > Securities & Parties > Parties.
b- Search for the Broker, if not found then make a new Party.
c- Fill in the broker name in the following fields: Code, Name, and Broker, then save


4- Advice the user on the below steps:
a- Change the issuer in the security to match with the broker in the order. In the above example, the issuer in the security needs to be changed from BARC to JPM. 
b - Proceed with send to accounting. 
c- Change the Broker in the order to the needed Issuer (in our example to BARC) and push that order through.

Error Case 3: OSTA Failed - Unable to Process Tax lot Allocation Designation due to insufficient Tax lot inventory 

Steps:
Open the order in CRD → Right Click → Navigation → Positions. 
Check all the details in the Lot Inventory Manager, Strategy, Substrategy, Custodian, etc.. and compare them with the Allocation. 
In the above example, the reason for the error was the non matching values under Custodian. 
Reach out to the user stating the correct value to use.
While waiting for the user's reply you can also check previous orders under the same Ticker, from the allocations on these orders you should see the correct value. 
Based on your findings, update the mismatching values. 
Trade date in tax lots being incorrect can also be the reason behind the error.
If none of the above works, create a new tax lot. It will automatically set the settle's date for today's date and copy the information you have from the position. Delete the old position and push to accounting.


Error Case 4: STA Failed - Unassigned Privilege 



Steps:

In CR, navigate to Sys Admin >> User Administration >> Command Privileges.Navigate to the privilege: Send to Accounting Command in Process Server. If it's set to Unassigned, change it to Grant. This will allow the user to use the MCM Send to Accounting button.

Error Case 5: STA Failed - Deleted Allocation (No longer found present)



Steps:

Go to Import/Export Translation Tables >> TAXLOT_USER ALLOCATION Table >> Search for the combination from the screenshot.If its not found, then it was already deleted based on previous instructions.
Error Case 6: Unexpected Server Response, Response Status Code 401


In case of the above error, instruct user to restart Charles River and report back if issue persists. 


Error Case 7: Unable to Send to Accounting 



Steps:

Sometimes, even when an order is fully filled, the Done Quantity may be missing at the allocation level. Please check whether the Done field is populated.Manually entering a value in the Done field will not resolve the issue.To correct it, advise the user to clone the order, then send the new order to Accounting. The original order should be cancelled.
Error Case 8: MCM Done Error



Steps:

This error is a known issue with swap orders received via FIX, which can conflict with Charles River’s Auto-Allocation feature due to a workflow glitch.Right click on the order, select Actions, then click Allocate. Once allocation is completed, retry the original action the user was attempting.
Error Case 9: Missing Mandatory Purpose Code (usrClassCd4)

This error usually arises when Moore are trading SWAP securities.This field is under the “Other” tab in the order entry, named ORD Text 4. Send the user the dropdown and ask them to populate the correct value, then try to send to accounting.

This document addresses errors that may occur when using the MCM Custom buttons. Several types of errors can arise, their corresponding solutions are outlined below.

Error Cases:
Error Case 1: MCM Done Failed - Unable to connect to the remote serverError Case 2: Issuer Assigned to Counterparty does not match the Issuer Assigned to the SecurityError Case 3: OSTA Failed - Unable to Process Tax lot Allocation Designation due to insufficient Tax lot inventory Error Case 4: STA Failed - Unassigned Privilege Error Case 5: STA Failed - Deleted Allocation (No longer found present)Error Case 6: Unexpected Server Response, Response Status Code 401Error Case 7: Unable to sent to accountingError Case 8: MCM Done ErrorError Case 9: Missing Mandatory Purpose Code (usrClassCd4)`

Subject: Weekly CRD Restart Checks - Sunday July 2nd, 2023
Body:
Hello Maher and Andrew,
Please note that the weekly restart completed normally. All CRD services are running and triggers and audit parameters are enabled.

Every Sunday, we initiate a restart of the Moore CRD servers for three environments: QA, UAT, and PROD. This process occurs at 12:30 am EST, which corresponds to 8:30 AM Dubai time, facilitated by Tidal jobs. The jobs are expected to conclude by 12:50 am EST, and subsequently, we anticipate receiving an email confirming the successful completion of the jobs. (Please note the time changes during daylight savings)
To ensure that the jobs have been completed successfully and that all the necessary services are active and running, we need to conduct several checks before 3 am EST which is 11 am Dubai time.
To execute the weekend restart checks, follow the below steps:
Check that we receive the success/failure emails for the weekly restart job group. These emails should be sent to charlesriversupport@moorecap.com. We will be receiving three emails, one for each environment (QA/UAT, PROD). Example emails are seen in the screenshots below.
QA/UAT: The email is sent from SchedulerQA@moorecap.com.
PROD: The email is sent from Scheduler@moorecap.com.`

Missing Files: This alert is most likely because the brokers didn’t send their files. In this case, Patrick Pingaro will reach out to the brokers and ask them to drop their files. we can rerun the tidal job corresponding to that broker (pulling FTP).Open f8.PNGFiles With Bad Dates: This alert is most likely due to Tidal Jobs pulling a file from a previous day. This also means that the primebrokers didn’t send their files for the day.
