- `Example Troubleshooting Scenarios Sample Scenarios and Walkthrough for Tidal Job Troubleshooting Tidal Job Stakeholders If we don't know who the stakeholders are for a job, ask Victor Raber and Ed Mullady (US) or Mark Buckthorp (Dubai) in the shared Teams chat. Explaining why it is advisable to check the audit logs versus checking job status from main window: Sometimes the main window can show if a job completed abnormally or normally as well as have other messages. Many jobs, however, after completion, return to a waiting state making the main window not the place to confirm if a job ran normally or not, requiring that we check from the Audit Log tab when clicking details. Job Name and Error Action Escalation Contacts Completed Abnormally / 130_W_MSCI_BARRA_LOAD_EFMUSALT Escalate Immediately Alena.Alasheeva@moorecap.com Sandeep.Grewal@moorecap.com Michael.Litvin@moorecap.com RMUsers@moorecap.com

The first troubleshooting steps revolve around ensuring that jobs are not stuck; if a job is stuck, then we need to assess why and then work to un-stick it.

[Section A] For any Tidal Job Alert, first identify whether the job is stuck:
Login to Tidal PROD at URL: us1-es03:8080Use your Moore Windows credentials to log in, except add "win\" to your username, e.g., win\omni_jungerIn the Job Activity view, search for the job in the bar on the top right. For example:Open the Job Details window by either double-clicking on the Job or by right-clicking and then selecting Details.Select the "Audit Log" Tab and click "View Archive Logs" (note: needing to click 'View Archive Logs' to see the Audit Log contents is a bug. The Audit Log will load without clicking this button once the bug is fixed.)Review the Audit Log to determine whether the job is stuck in a failed state.A job is healthy if it is in 'Completed Normally' status or reruns itself after a failure and then goes into 'Completed Normally' status. 
A job is stuck if we received an email alert for it and:
it is in 'Errored Occurred', 'Completed Abnormally', 'Agent Unavailable', or 'Orphaned' status.it is in 'Timed Out' status, with some exceptions.If you are not sure if a job is an exception, treat it as though it is in 'Completed Abnormally' status.it is in Active status for significantly longer than the anticipated run duration.If you are not sure how long "significantly long" is, escalate internally.per the Audit Log, it reruns itself and a majority of the executions result in 'Completed Abnormally' status since the failures began.In other words, as a rule of thumb, if a job fails, reruns itself, and then succeeds, the job is still considered stuck unless it succeeds for a second time. If a job fails twice in a row, then succeeds twice in a row, it is still stuck until is succeeds for a third time.If an alternating pattern of success and failures emerges, while that means the job is not stuck, it does suggest that the underlying service may be unhealthy. This should be escalated to Tidal Techs.[Section B] If the job is stuck:
If the job is safe to rerun:Note: If you are not sure whether the job is safe to rerun, skip to Step B.2Right-click on the job -> Job Control -> Rerun. BE CAUTIOUS to rerun ONLY the target job and no others when prompted.Wait for the job to run and repeat Step A.5 to ensure the job Completed Normally. If the job still Completes Abnormally, then proceed to Step B.2If the job is not safe to rerun, or rerunning did not work:Review the "Output" tab and inspect the contents to determine the cause of the failure. The "Output" tab's contents are also captured in the email alert that was triggered by the job failure.Cross-reference your analysis of the "Output" tab with existing documentation and past tickets to determine the next course of action.If the next course of action is not obvious, escalate internally. If the next course of action is obvious, but the job failure is novel, escalate internally. 

If internal escalation fails, escalate to stakeholders and share findings thus far. From there, we will collaborate with Moore to seek a resolution.

For additional background information, please see Introduction to Tidal.

The Support Desk frequently receives Tidal Job Alert tickets, which come in the form of an email alert from Scheduler@moorecap.com. There are other Tidal email alerts that will be covered in their own articles: (BORPTCHECK alerts and IQ ERROR alerts).

The email subject often looks like this: EOD CRITICAL ALERT: CHARLES_RIVER / Completed Abnormally / 

And the email body will look something like this:

- `FIX Log LocationsWhen logged into a Soros virtual workstation, open File Explorer to access the FIX message logs at the below locations:
Prod File Location: \\vsompcrv05\logs\cameron_buy.log
Archived Prod Logs: \\nyc-sor-vlog-01\Logs\CharlesRiver\ProdV17\FIX
UAT File Location: \\vrchdcrv01\CharlesRiver\17_mt1\serverapps\logs
Soros moves FIX logs that are more than 3 days old into an Archive folder. If you don’t see the logs for the date that you need in the Prod file location, they were likely moved to the Archive folder.

Accessing FIX logviewer.batNavigate to the following location inside of the UAT Middle tier:
C:\CharlesRiver\17_mt1\serverapps\fix\bin\logviewer.bat

Copying the FIX LogsWhenever you are viewing FIX logs, it is important to copy them to a local location first before viewing. Otherwise, if you open them directly, the FIX Engine will no longer be able to write to them, which can cause catastrophic breaks.

There two ways we can copy the FIX logs to a safe, location:
Via Command Prompt
Via File Explorer

Copying Via File ExplorerOpen File Explorer
Type in the FIX Log Location that you need.
Then copy the cameron_buy.log file and paste it to your virtual workstation in a location of your preference. For example, you may paste it directly to your Desktop for quick access and then move it to a more organized subfolder later. 
Copying Via Command PromptThis method has a few extra steps due to the fact that we can’t access our user working folders from the command prompt (this is because they are on a separate network drive). However, this method is safer than the above since you don’t run the risk of mis-clicking when attempting to copy the file.
From the Start Menu on your Soros virtual workstation, launch Command Prompt
Identify your preferred file location.
In the example below, the preferred file location is the 'Documents' folder, i.e., C:\User\ \Documents
Execute the following command: copy \\ /Y 
In this example, the command is 'copy \\vsompcrv05\logs\cameron_buy.log /Y C:\Users\torrek\Documents'
The target environment is Production, so we access 'vsompcrv05'. 
We want the latest FIX logs, so we target 'cameron_buy.log'. Prior days' logs will be appended with the date (e.g. 'cameron_buy.log.06-25-2025'). 
Kathleen Torres is the user in this example, so she is using her own Documents folder for the destination folder. 
The '/Y' flag suppresses prompts that would confirm whether you want to overwrite existing files in the destination folder.
You can learn more about the 'copy' command by searching online for 'Windows Commands copy'.

To make the logs accessible for viewing in the UAT middle tier, you will need to then copy and paste this file into your User Working folder.

- `FIX LogsSoros FIX Logs can be obtained by running a simple script from the command line of your virtual workstation: How to copy FIX Logs to local machine

FIX Log ViewerSoros has given us access to the Fix log viewer outside of their MTs for ease of review when trying to troubleshoot regardless of environment. 
This can be located under \\nyc-sor-vlog-01\Logs\CharlesRiver\UAT1\FIXViewer\serverapps\fix\bin\fixlogviewer.bat
This version of the Log viewer is older and the timestamps will be Machine Time, not the Sending Time as reported on the message, so all times are Eastern.



Common DetailsSoros completes both Solicited and Unsolicited Fix trading. They do not have any unsolicited Equity Trading. They have both Solicited and Unsolicited Fixed Income trading and represents a majority of their unsolicited business.

CRD FIX ConnectionsSome of the FIX connections are hosted by Charles River, but CRD does not have access to their FIX environment to manage any of the FIX connections directly. However, CRD can still be contacted on behalf of Soros if we need their assistance with anything.
Soros’s CRD FIX connections are not frequently used except after-hours, so have very little traffic. These connections very rarely go down, but if they do, we need to check if they need to be reconnected.

Non-CRD FIX ConnectionsSoros primarily uses FLEX FIX connections. These connections rarely go down but if they do, we need to check if they need to be reconnected. 

FLEX ConnectionsThe following FIX connections are important. We will receive direct alerts if one of the following connections goes DOWN. If they go down, it is critically important that they be brought back up as soon as possible
FIX_CONN_NAME
TARGET_COMP_ID
ZFLEXEMS
FLEXEMS
FLEXFX
FLEXFX
FLEXFI
RPTS_FI
They are scheduled to go down at 5:30PM EST Monday to Friday. They are scheduled to come back up Sunday to Thursday at 5:45PM EST. In other words, they are automatically bounced between US market close and Asia market open.

FIX FLEX AlertThe email shows the name of the connection, TargetCompID, SenderCompID, connection status, ScheduledStart, and ScheduledStop.
These alerts are fired only when the connection comes down not at a scheduled time.

Sender of the email: CRFIX@soros.com

FIX FLEX Alert During US HolidaysAlthough FIX connections can at times be expected to go down during Holidays, a check should be done for the FIX Flex Alert to make sure this is expected behavior. Email or call Flex Trade with information below to confirm status of connection. 
Escalate to Soros and Internal parties to keep them informed of the current situation if this is confirmed to be an unexpected disconnection.

FIX Troubleshooting
FLEXIf a FLEX connection goes down mid-day, we need to immediately contact FLEX to resolve the issue:
Michael.Madden@flextrade.com (Soros Client Relationship Contact)Jonathan.Durinda@flextrade.com (Soros Client Relationship Contact)Alexandra.Falkner@flextrade.com (Developer)flextradersupport@flextrade.comFXSupport@flextrade.com (only if FX line is down)FlexFX-Project-soros@flextrade.com (only if FX line is down)

Ensure #ExecutionTech@soros.com is also looped in.

Send an email to the above contacts as soon as possible, and then start calling the following numbers if we do not receive a response within 5 minutes:
Support - 516-627-8993Michael Madden - 646-961-3479Jonathan Durinda - 347-270-8475

GeneralSoros has a job that runs every 5 minutes that will automatically pull the connections back up if they are down. If anything is wrong and for some reason the job is unable to bring the connection back up, then we may need to reconnect it manually and investigate the blockage. 

Additionally, there is a script that automatically resubmits FIX messages that did not go through, whether due to an outage or other known issues. FIX messages that need to be resubmitted (i.e., stuck in the queue), will fire alerts to the affected user(s) until the issue is resolved. Typically, the user tries to fix it themselves before raising to the Support Desk.

Re-Fetch Security
Soros does have RTAPI configured and connected to Bloomberg and they have the capability of re-fetching if the security type is one they pull information from Bloomberg with. Keep in mind that Fix errors tied to the security should be reviewed and vetted where possible before suggesting a re-fetch.

Manually Book
It is a rare circumstance for them to manually book because they ideally have their automations configured to allow for that to be inserted in and thus Manually booking is viewed as a last resort.
If the Fix Message itself has incorrect tag entries and they are not something we have any control over. We get the users agreement to manually book and then clear the Fix message from the resubmit queue.If there is any issue with a security we are not able to isolate the problem within a timely fashion that we can advise them to alter the security type to a more simpler version, should this fail...we'd then suggest for a manual booking.In all instances, root cause identification is still required.`

- `How-to1. Rerun ETL TaskThere is no option to run Task/Subtask from ETL front-end. The best way to run to locate the Autosys job and kickoff there. For key nightly ETL tasks, you can find the Autosys job here - Jennison Overnight Support Runbook
When Supporting failed job,
If you “Force Complete” before run, ETL will rerun from the first subtask
If you do not “Force Complete” before run, ETL will rerun from the failed subtask
2. Rerun certain ETL SubtaskSometimes, you may need just run few, but not all subtask from a given task. Follow below steps -
Go to ETL Dashboard Task Details (Subtask) view
Uncheck jobs you want to skip from “Enable” column
Click “Save Task”
Kick-off associated Autosys job to rerun ETL task. Disable subtask will be skipped.
Open image-20240911-080419.png

IntroductionSummaryETL is a Jennison in-house developed task execution tool. It supports DB operation, file generation/watch/deliver, scripts executing and calling API. It also allow user to manage job dependency and execution concurrency (e.g. X and Y cannot run at the same time). ETL task/subtask are behind most of FIAPPS Autosys jobs, and there is an on-going effort to migrate jobs from ETL to Autosys.
 
Job TypesETL define jobs on two levels
Task- It acts as a logical group of all underlying subtasks. Task is not executable, it is similar to “box” concept in Autosys
Subtask- Executable job. It has several operation types and each support different set of command/parameter. Most (if not all) FIAPPS subtasks fall into below types
Extract - Extract data from Oracle DB and produce output flat file
Extract_Delivery - Deliver file from source to destination folder
Executable - Call scripts or API
Interface - Call Oracle DB store proc
StageLoader - Load file into Oracle DB, following mapping instructions from control file
File Retrieval - Monitor source folder, when available, move file to destination folder. Support file rename.
Job StatusETL flag task/subtask with 1 of the 7 status
Open image-20240911-072951.png#
Status
Apply To
Description
1
Completed
Task/Subtask
Job is completed without issue
2
Skipped
Subtask
Job is not executed per user instruction
3
Not Run
Task/Subtask
Job is defined but there is no run history
4
Failed
Task/subtask
Job failure
5
Warning
??
Has not seen this happened
6
InProgress
Task/Subtask
Job is running
7
Force Complete
Task/Subtask
Job failed and user killed it
SemaphoreETL uses semaphore to manage resource and prevent concurrency. User follow below steps to defined semaphore
Identify jobs that cannot be running at the same time, normally due to resource constrain (e.g. calling the same scripts or stored proc)
Create a semaphore, give it a meaningful name
Assign the semaphore to all tasks that uses the same resource
Now when more than 1 tasks with the same semaphore are running. ETL will queue-up jobs and let them complete one by one
Job Naming ConventionSimilar to Autosys, ETL task/subtask uses prefix to define group ownership. Here are few prefix we need to pay attention
CRD/JENN_CRD - Mostly CRD import/export related job, nightly or intraday
FIDATA - Benchmark processing, Pricing/Analytic processing
REMOVE - Old naming convention for decommissioned job
CRD_UNSCHEDULED - Decommissioned FIAPPS job (we normally don’t delete jobs from ETL)
 
ETL ScreensETL Dashboard
Task ViewThis view shows all the task and their status.
The default list order is by job status - InProgress → Failed → Not Run → Force complete → Completed
Column header support various search criteria
User can click individual Task name to open Subtask View
Open image-20240911-074540.pngSubtask ViewThis view shows subtask that belong to select task and their status. User can also review the historical run status for the task
User can click individual Subtask name from the top right panel to open the Subtask Details View
User can click individual Task name from the lower right panel to open Subtask View of historical runs
Open image-20240911-074922.pngSubtask Details ViewMost jobs with Executable type has detailed log on this view
Open image-20240911-075044.pngSubTaskUser can Select Task/Subtask and view/edit job setting
Open image-20240911-075230.pngMain ETL TablesDatabase: JDSP7

- `Identifying Environment Downtime
Symptoms
Standard login error:"The server cannot log you in. The server may be down, the server name might be incorrect or missing port number. Please check and try again."

This often occurs if the environment is down or misconfigured.

Quick Check via Browser
Open a browser.In the address bar, type: http:// If you receive:Expected error (e.g. page can't load): the server responded but is down.Connection refused or unreachable: the server might be offline.For a more specific endpoint test, append: /crts/mainmenu.doExample: http://CR03/crtsnts/mainmenu.do 
 2. Remote Desktop into Middle Tier Server

Accessing the Server
Open Remote Desktop Connection.Type the server name (e.g., US2UATCR03).Click Connect.Use More choices to select the proper Windows credentials.Post Login
After login, you can validate if services are running and available via Services(mentioning services here)


 3. Validate via Charles River Web Admin

Accessing Web Admin
Navigate to the Master Cluster’s Web Admin.Check for live middle tier entries.If missing, confirm that the middle tier services are actually running.Availability Tab
Navigate to the Availability section.It will show:Which middle tiers are expected.Their current status (e.g. Inactive, Active).This helps identify any missing or non-functional middle tier servers.

4. Check Middle Tier Configuration (Install Directory)

Locate Install Path
On the middle tier server (via Remote Desktop), go to: C:\Program Files\ \serverapps\If multiple services exist (e.g., each in its own folders), check each one individually.5. Run CRD_configure.bat to Validate Configuration

Steps
Navigate to: \bin\Locate: CRD_configure.batRight-click > Run as AdministratorCluster Configuration
Go to the Utilities tab > Select Cluster Configuration.Confirm the following:JMS Configuration: (e.g., Artemis)
Current Middle Tier Instance
Master Cluster Member (should match the actual master)
Ports (e.g., 661616, 2099)
Member Discovery: set to Database
If the middle tier is misconfigured or not connecting:

Refresh Middle Tier Connection
Click Current Middle Tier (sets itself as the master temporarily).Then manually re-select the correct master (e.g., CR03).Click Save.This refreshes the local cache and can resolve connection or sync issues.6. Recap: What This Training Covered

How to verify if a Charles River environment is down.
How to check reachability via browser endpoints.
How to use Remote Desktop to access middle tier servers.
How to validate and view middle tier status using the Web Admin tool.
How to locate and check Charles River installation folders.
How to run CRD_configure.bat and re-point to the correct master cluster.
How to resolve common port binding and cache issues.

7. Final Step: Retry Login

Once all checks and configuration steps are completed:

Attempt to log back into Charles River.If successful, the environment is back up and operational.

This guide outlines the steps to bring up a Charles River environment when it is down, including how to verify middle tier status, access web admin tools, and reconfigure middle tiers using CRD_configure.bat.`
- `Identify the Problematic Security:
Review the error message in MWB to locate the security causing the issue.
Open the Security in Charles River:
Navigate to the security master and search for the impacted security.
Check the "Local Currency" Field:
Verify the value in the Local Currency field.
If it is set to NLG, confirm if this is incorrect by comparing with similar securities.
Review Security History:
Check the audit/history logs to confirm when and how the currency was altered.
Correct the Currency:
Update the Local Currency from NLG to EUR (or the appropriate valid currency based on context).
Save the changes.
Validation:
Reload the MWB to ensure it loads successfully for all users.
Confirm that no further currency-related errors appear.
Communicate Resolution:
Notify impacted users that MWB is operational.
Document the change in internal logs for future reference.

In Charles River's Manager Workbench (MWB), users encountered a critical issue where the MWB failed to load for all users. The error displayed indicated:"Security (XXXX) relies on Currency (NLG) for LOC_CRNNCY_CD, but that Currency is invalid: CRRNCY_SEC_ID: 0"
This issue occurs when a security references an invalid or incorrect local currency code. In this case, the currency was set to NLG, which is no longer valid. Typically, similar securities use EUR as the local currency. Upon reviewing the security's history, it was found that the currency was originally EUR but was mistakenly changed to NLG by the system.
As MWB depends on valid security configurations to load properly, any such invalid reference will prevent access for all users, requiring immediate action.`

- `If we are receiving these alerts every minute, we should be able to identify which environment they are originating from. In this particular case, the alert came from CRD-QA.

Note: If the alert is coming from PROD, please immediately contact Prasad and CC Mark and Maher in the email. Do not stop or interfere with any scripts running in PROD without prior approval, as this could impact critical processes.

Steps
1. Log in to the SQL Server:
Server: SQL-CRD-UAT
Credentials: Use the CRD Admin account (as Windows authentication does not show running scripts).
Database: CRD_QA
Username: CRDADMIN
Password: crd****n (Please ask a senior team member for the full credentials.)
2. Run the following query to check for open transactions: SELECT * FROM sys.sysprocesses WHERE open_tran = 1;

3. Before terminating any transaction, carefully review what is currently running. Ensure that it is safe to proceed, as terminating the wrong process could disrupt active operations.



4. To kill the transaction:
Identify the SPID from the alert (e.g., for this alert it was 675).
Run the following command: KILL 675


5. Verify resolution:
Re-run the query from step 2: SELECT * FROM sys.sysprocesses WHERE open_tran = 1;
Ensure there are no remaining open transactions.
Wait two minutes to confirm that no new alerts are coming in.
If the alerts have stopped, reply to the original email to confirm that the issue has been resolved.

When To Use This?

Please note that the alert mentioned below does not appear in Halo; instead, it is sent directly to our Support inbox. If this alert is indicative of an ongoing issue, we will continue receiving it every minute until the problem is resolved. If the Support Inbox is not visible in your Outlook, please follow the steps on this page to add it: Adding Support Inbox into Outlook.`
- `If you notice that the connection status is red (disconnected) instead of yellow , follow these steps: Check the FIX Console in UAT WebAdmin : Ensure the FIX Console is started . If it is not, click Start . This often resolves the issue. If the issue persists , continue with the steps below: Go to Passwordstate → us2uat-cr06 → Services . Check if the FIX engine is running . If it is not, right-click and select Start . Restart the required services in the following order : Restart CrdFIXScheduler first. Then restart CrdFIXMonitor . Verify both services are running : If either service is not running, manually start them again in the same order: CrdFIXScheduler CrdFIXMonitor Once both services are up, go back to the FIX Console and attempt to reconnect the connections using: Connect and Reset → Execute

- `In Bloomberg, a “Yellow Key” is a colored function key used to define the type of financial instrument you’re working with. Pressing a yellow key after typing a ticker tells Bloomberg what asset class the ticker belongs to. 

When there is mention of a yellow key, it generally refers to the security Type that is usually added the end of a Ticker or other identifier to identify a security type, and also the underlying security type.

For example, and index future will generally have an identifier or ticker ending with Index to identify the future is an index future. same for equity and commodity.`

- `Is Outlook disconnected? Check the bottom of your Outlook window

If you see Disconnected, Working Offline, or Trying to connect…, Outlook can't reach the email server to send your mail.
Choose Send/Receive > Preferences > Work Offline to toggle between offline mode and online mode. You may need to open the message and send it again, or choose Send/Receive.

Make sure you're connected to the Internet
If you lose your connection to the internet, Outlook won't be able to send your messages.
Open a browser, such as Internet Explorer, Edge, or Chrome.
Try entering one of the following websites in the address bar:
http://www.microsoft.com
http://www.bing.com
If you can't reach either of those two websites, see Troubleshoot network connectivity problems in Internet Explorer.
Do your messages contain large attachments?Some email providers (such as your ISP or even Gmail) won't let you send messages larger than a certain size. Adding pictures and large attachments to your message can prevent you from sending that message (and any messages sent after the large message).
Go to your Outbox and then open the stuck email messages. Start with the oldest message first. If that message has an attachment that’s larger than 5MB, delete the attachment and either attach a smaller file or attach a link to the attachment. See Attach a file to an email in Outlook for more information on attachments.
Now try to send the message again. If that doesn’t fix the problem, see the next section.

If you see messages piling up in your Outlook outbox or people you're sending messages to aren't receiving them, try the following methods to fix the issue. We've presented the methods in order of quickest to most in-depth.`

- `Job Scheduling During FI Holidays Please note that jobs will not run during the following FI Holidays : Columbus Day Veterans Day – Monday, November 11th Troubleshooting: If you notice a job is not running, you can check whether its start condition has been met. By tracing the start condition, you can eventually identify the job with a scheduled calendar that may be affecting its execution. See ticket: 45757

MKTData Job Monitoring Procedure
1. Job Start Time
Check if the MKTData job started at 1:30 AM ET.
2. Job Completion Time
If the job completes within 10 minutes of starting, this is considered a failure. Please escalate to Tristan via Teams and a call immediately.
If the job is running, it should complete within 45 minutes or more.
3. Newprice File Check
Once the job starts running, verify that the Newprice file has dropped at 1:30 AM.
File Path: \\jallc.lan\data\marketdata\prod\yieldbook\data 
If the Newprice file is not present, please escalate to Tristan via Teams and a call.



4. File Content Validation
Open the Newprice file and confirm that it contains today's date.
For Monday runs, the file should reflect the date from two days prior (Saturday's date), which is expected.


5. Post-Completion ActionsOnce the MKTData job completes:
The Newpriceoutz file will be archived in the Archive folder.
The rdc_fi_yb_analytics_load_check job will begin running, which typically takes around 5 minutes to complete.

Summary of the process: 
 
AutoSys Job
mktdata_fi_analytics_yieldbook_execute
 
Job start time is 1:30 am eastern. Generates a file called “newprice” (no extension) in \\jallc.lan\data\marketdata\prod\yieldbook\data and sends it to YieldBook for processingProcessing takes about 45 minutes and should complete around 2:15am easternYieldBook will return a file called newpriceoutz.1.yyyymmdd which will get staged for Eagle / RDC to process 
rdc_fi_yb_analytics_load_check
Job is triggered once the market data job has completedThis will load the staged data to RDC 
Support Needed
We would like for you to verify a couple of things to ensure that the process is running as expected. 
At 1:30am check the file that is saved to the market data folder. Open the file in notepad, and check two things: i. The 10 digit date in the first row matches current day “20250910”
 ii. The settle date in the far right column also has the same date “20250910”

In the even that the settle date (2nd row) has a different date, please email Joe Brown and Jared Sussman to confirm if the dates are correct. See ticket for reference: 55374
For the First BD of the month we will see the file rever back to the first day of the month. See ticket for reference: 57259

 
Verify that the yield book response file is generated sometime around 2:15am. The list of securities between the request file and response file should be very close. Make sure that the market data job completes successfullyMake sure that the rdc job completes successfully 
**** Note. Market data job takes roughly 45 minutes. If it takes 10 minutes that’s a fail. 
 
If the jobs file or the files do not include the correct dates, please call me at 214-208-0774.`

- `Navigate to Instrument Default. (Under Setup → Trading)
Check the available instrument defaults to see if one is set up for the instrument you’re investigating or experiencing an issue with. 
For example, in the mentioned ticket, an issue was experienced with a FRA, specifically ZAR.
So we would start by searching for instrument defaults that include either “ZAR” or “FRA” in the name, until you locate the one you’re looking for or can confirm its not setup.
If you can locate the instrument default, first check that the determinants match the values in the issue you’re investigating (Ex: it would have a rule to specify FRA and a rule to specify ZAR). Then, review the instrument defaults table below and verify the value assigned to the field that isn’t populating correctly, to ensure it’s set up properly (Ex: we’re expecting to find under Ref Index, the value the user was looking for).
If you can’t locate it, it likely hasn’t been set up yet and will need to be created by adding the determinants you need and the values you expect in the instrument defaults table (Create a new one by clicking on new in the same window shown above and filling the fields).

Background

Fields are sometimes expected to populate automatically based on the defaults configured in the Instrument Defaults section. This feature allows users to define the default/standard settings for instruments such as swaps, swaptions, FRAs, repos, options. These defaults help streamline workflows related to order entry and security setup by reducing manual input. 

In the Instrument Defaults window:
The Determinants (top section) define the conditions for when a default rule applies.
The Instrument Defaults table (bottom section) lists the actual field values that will be auto-filled when those conditions are met.



Problem

- `Operator Release: 
it means that the job will not run automatically, even if all its scheduling conditions and dependencies are met. Instead, it enters a "Held" or "Waiting for Operator Release" state, and must be manually released by an operator to execute.

This is typically used when:
﻿﻿You want manual control over when a job runs.﻿﻿The job performs sensitive operations (e.g., affecting production data).﻿﻿It's part of a critical chain, and you want to pause the flow until someone confirms it's safe to proceed.﻿﻿You're in a testing or maintenance phase and want to avoid automatic runs.
The Operator Release check is found in the "Job Definition" > "Options" as a checkbox with the name "Require operator release":`
- `Order Creation Steps:

Select one of 4 options: Buy, Sell, Cover and Short from the menu bar (it can be customized)an Order Form opens that requires certain information:Now that the order shows in the blotter with a prelim status, several things to note from the Order History Tab:Next Step is to run the compliance check by right clicking > "Actions" > "Check Compliance" on order or clicking "Check Compliance" on the order form menu items. This checks against the compliance rules. If a condition is met for any rule, the "Alert, Warnings and Data Exception Rule Window will open, and the order Status will change to Pre-Warn or Pre-Alert. In the text case, we got a Pre-Alert:If the alert is resolved by an approval, the order will automatically be changed to Pre-OK. If the change is manual, after saving and running the Compliance Check, the status should change to PreOk.Additional info appears on the Order History, where it can be seen that that after receiving the compliance alert, and the account changed, the compliance gave no alert and moved trade to PreOK:Next step is to Send To Trading (right-click on order > "Actions" > "Send To Trading") to make the order ready to be executed by having status = "New". To start execution, a trader should take ownership:Once a trader takes ownership (right-click on order > "Actions" > "Take Ownership", we also see some changes on the Order History:The trader is set.Order status changes OpenThe 3rd line with MARSOMS sets ????? Need to know what that is.After the order is in Open Status, we need to add placement to the order. Right-click on the order > "Actions" > "Place/Fill". This sends the order to Work Status, and add additional info to the Order History:The broker in the instance above has been set to BARCP, and we can see the placement added and should appear in the placement window.Once the order is in Work Status, we can add a fill. It is the same process; right-click > "Actions" > "Place/Fill". add the Fill price and save:This window also includes the send/replace through FIX, which is the section between the placement and fill windows in blue. Once saved, we can see further updates in the Order History window:

Creating an order is CRD goes through several steps before the order is completed and added to the books for reconciliation. For Moore specifically, the CRD:
Order Creation: Prelim (requires allocation to be created), PreOk, Pre-Warn, Pre-AlertOrder Trading: New, Open, Work, Ready, Acct,`
- `Other fields that will contain execution broker info are:

This feed could be referencing a broker that then determines a different exec broker.Order Placement should be also checked: If we are not sure how Charles River treats placement with different brokers (splits them or does not split) then checking the exec broker filed on the order placement table can include exec brokers missed if we only search for the Exec Brokers in the order table.Include order_sec_spec as it is an extension of the order table and has exec broker info.

We received a request to search for all exec_brokers for Soros from 1/1/2025. Although exec_broker is a field on the order, it may not cover all execution brokers. Out search needs to include other fields that may have exec broker information.`

cd ../download
ls (to double check if the files have been moved to the directory)

Copy the file from drop off download directory to the US1-PROC02 primebroker’s incoming directory using the pscp command in the Copy.bat script. (Highlighted below)
Remove the files from the drop off download directory by copying the second plink statement in Copy.bat script. (Highlighted below)
Run the Move.pl script by copying/executing the below statement in Copy.bat script. (Highlighted below)

- `Preprocess Warning:
These steps should only be undertaken if there is not already any existing FIX connection for the given broker.
Check inside Web Admin to see if broker code already existsCheck inside of Charles River as well to assure FIX Connection does not already existExample Email:
1. Gather Session Details
Get all information above from the broker (session details) and the confirm the information needed from Moore.Provide the Broker with the external IP address so they can whitelist it: 207.223.48.0/20 (check with Christopher Pitera or Moore support).2. Telnet check 
To check if the IP address is working and connecting:
Go to the correct FIX middle tier and open a command prompt (cmd)Check if Telnet is successful by writing a command in the following format:telnet (ip_address) (port)Telnet is working if a new window appears like below:Telnet has failed if "Connect failed" has shown:3. Shutdown FIX Server
Go to Web AdminShutdown FIX Server4. Stop FIX Engine
Connect to the proper FIX middle tier through PasswordstateGo to Services and stop Charles River QuickFixJ Engine Service:5. Stunnel (If connection does not use Stunnel this can be skipped)
Connect to the proper FIX middle tier through PasswordstateOpen Command Prompt (cmd)Enter Command seen in step 2 with following format: telnet (ip_address) (port)This will open new telnet window, you are doing this to check that connection can be established with IP address and port associated with new connection. Open a new command prompt (cmd) windowWrite "netstat" to show all local addressesWrite following command: netstat -ano | find (port)This command sees if a port is in use or notSearch for unused ports with following structure '9xxx' to find available portsOn FIX Middle Tier go to directory where STunnel config file (stunnel.conf) is kept. C:\Program Files(x86)\stunnel\configUpdate Stunnel Configuration with new connection informationFirst line is the name of the connection between brackets ( [TradeWebSwapsUS] )Accept is the open port you identified through using netstatConnect is the IP address and port number that were given from broker and used on telnet check earlierCAfile is the certificate location: Certificate must be placed at file path destination. Certificate is generally given by brokerIn cases that broker does not given certificate we use the public one shown here:Example entry:6. Create a New FIX Connection in Web Admin
After logging into Web Admin go to Cluster>FIX Console>New ConnectionEnter the session details provided by the brokerName can be selected be usAll other details must match exactly as given by the broker or connection could failIf Stunnel was used, change the following details:Set IP Address of connection to 127.0.0.1Set port to be the port number you found using netstatOnce all necessary information is entered hit "Create"7. Restart Windows Services
If Stunnel configuration was altered, restart STunnel TLS Wrapper ServiceStart Charles River QFJ Engine Service (Same service you stopped earlier)8. Start FIX Server Again and Bring Up New Connection
After logging into Web Admin, go to Cluster> FIX ConsoleHit "Start" to bring server back upClick on "Manage" to enter menu where status of FIX Connections is shown:Find the corresponding connection you created and checkmark the box next to it that is under the "Connect" columnGo back to the top of the page and hit the "Execute" buttonYour new connection should now be showing as connected9. Configure Timing of Connection in FIX Scheduler
On FIX Middle Tier, find the FIX Scheduler configuration file, "Schedule.txt". Can be found under the file path C:/FixAdminUtil/configOpen the file and an entry for the FIX connection that matches the date and time agreed upon with the broker. Should be setup in the same structure as the other entries, following the connection template which can be found on the first line.More details regarding this process can be found in the "Adding to the Scheduler" section of this knowledge base article: FIX Scheduler`

There is another way to know the broker’s original file name. Let’s say one of Goldman’s files was not good and we need to tell them that. First, we need to find the correct file name created by the prime brokers. In order to do that, we need to look at the script that ran during the FTP process. 
Go to Tidal → RECON (under job activity) → REPORTS → RECON → RECON_DAILY_BATCH → PB_RECON_FILE_RETRIEVAL → The job of the specific prime broker (in our case above, it is Goldman).
Check the file the job is running in the Dependencies section.
 Go to US1-PROC02 Desktop → K Recon Folder → FTP .
In this folder you will find the .bat file that the job in Tidal is running to pull the prime brokers' files. In Goldman’s case, the job runs GSCopy.bat.
Open R21.PNG

Right click on the .bat file and select edit. We will find that the .bat is running a .pl script (here it is GSMove.pl)
Open R22.PNG
To access the .pl file, go to K Recon folder → Perl → Select the file and open it. 
Open R23.PNG
Once we open the file, we can see the prime broker file name and its corresponding Geneva name. For example, in line 71 in the below picture, the file sent by the prime broker is named posn.txt but Geneva technicians named it as GSPos. 
Open R24.PNG

The files we read from Recon are renamed by Geneva technicians. So, they don’t have the same name as sent by the brokers. 
The below table maps the name of the file in Recon to its name as sent by the brokers.
On the left hand side where it says Recon File Name, that is what Geneva technicians rename the file to and pull into Recon.
On the right hand side where it says Broker File Name, that is the name of the file that the brokers send directly to their FTP.`
- `PROD Archive ProcessAfter checking the access, sending the emails, you can start the archive. Make sure you are done with the archive before 1 pm (UK trading starts at that time).
Stop PROD Services
Log into us1-crd09 using PasswordState to stop PROD services and PROD master before running the archive.



Go to the following directory inside us1-crd09: C:\Users\NYCCRD\Desktop\Archive 21R2 Stop Scripts\PROD Stop Services Scripts




Stop PROD master and slave services
Run scripts 1 in command windows by double clicking on it.
When script 1 is done running, go to services and stop CharlesRiverUS1CRD098081 service.
 Run scripts 2 in command windows by double clicking on it.
2. Run Pre-Archive Batch Script
Log in to NYC-WS306 and run Archive_Before_PROD.bat found in: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive\Scripts
The script will set archive connectivity (in CRD), run table diagnostics (in both CRD and CRD_ARCHIVE), disable audit parameters (in both CRD and CRD_ARCHIVE), disable triggers (in CRD), get row counts, and save results in excel sheets under the following directory: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive. You should see the excel sheets in the directory, if you face any errors, contact Moore team (Zahraa Awad, Nour Hamdieh).







3. Run the Archive
Archive dry run:
Run “ArchiveAutomation” execution file found under the following directory: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive\Archive EXE\Iteration 1 by double clicking on it. The dry run (iteration = 1) is running the archive on 1 batch of orders to limit the errors (if any) on a specific number of orders.
If the archive got stuck on ALLOW_SNAPSHOT_ISOLATION, this indicates that we have open transactions which we need to kill. Use IdentifyingAndKillingSpids.sql found under X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive\Scripts to do so. 



We should receive an email from CRWebAdmin@moorecap.com sent to omv-moore-support@omnivistasolutions.com saying whether the run was successful or not


If we didn't receive the email within 10 minutes from the run finishing time, check the logs to make sure the run was successful.


if you get a duplication error, check this page : duplication error 
Archive Run:
If the dry run (iteration = 1) was successful, run “ArchiveAutomation” execution file found under the following directory: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive\Archive EXE\Iteration 0 by double clicking on it.




We should receive an email from CRWebAdmin@moorecap.com to omv-moore-support@omnivistasolutions.com saying whether the run was successful or not.


If the run finished with errors (will be mentioned in the email sent from CRWebAdmin@moorecap.com). Consult a member from Moore team in case of errors (Zahraa Awad, Nour Hamdieh)


4. Run Post-Archive Batch Script
Run Archive_After_PROD.bat found in: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive\Scripts
The script will run table diagnostics (in both CRD and CRD_ARCHIVE), enable audit parameters (in both CRD and CRD_ARCHIVE), enable triggers (in CRD), setup missing profiles, get row counts after the archive, compare pre-archive results to post archive results and generate the breaks, and save results in excel sheets under the following directory: X:\Technology\OmniAppSupport\Charles River\OMV\DBArchive\Automated PROD Archive. You should see the excel sheets in the directory, if you face any errors, contact Moore team (Zahraa Awad, Nour Hamdieh).
Issues with the files please look into this Halo ticket for resolution: Halo (ticket 21404) where only Row_Count_After document shows. (If we only see this sheet its okay to proceed.)
Check Row_Count_Breaks_yyyymmdd, Table_Diagnostics_CRD_Archive_Breaks_yyyymmdd, and Table_Diagnostics_CRD_Breaks_yyyymmdd. The excel sheets should be empty, if they have any records, contact Moore team (Zahraa Awad, Nour Hamdieh).




5. Start PROD Services 
If you have access to Tidal, rerun job group CRD_WEEKLY_RESTART.
If you have an issue with Tidal job that completed abnormally please see this halo ticket 21404 on how to solve the issue: Halo 
If you do not have access to Tidal:
Log into us1-crd09 using PasswordState to restart PROD services and PROD master.



Go to the following directory inside us1-crd09: C:\Users\NYCCRD\Desktop\Archive 21R2 Stop Scripts\PROD Stop Services Scripts



Start PROD master and slave services
Run scripts 3,4,5 and 6 in command windows. 
Open command windows, then drag and drop each script at a time and hit enter.
Restart us1-crd09 
On the us1-crd09, go to the start menu → Power → Restart. This will log you off from the machine.
Log back in into us1-crd09.
Start CRD services
Open the services window on us1-crd09 by pressing the services icon then select CharlesRiverUS1CRD098081 service, choose the start option. You should see status ”Running” once done. 




6. Do the weekly restart Checks Automated 21R2 Weekend Restart once CRD_WEEKLY_RESTART completes normally or the services have been started.

7. Send A Reply to the Outage Email
Once you complete the weekly restart checks and email, send an email saying that we are done with the PROD archive and mention that the servers are up and running.
Mention if the archive was successful or if we faced any issues that needs further debugging.

8. Notify the DBA’s

2. Paste all of the data you copied into the 'A' column.

- `Reference Ticket: 45823

Note: Always perform the steps in QA first, validate the fix, and then repeat in PROD. 

When using a shared blotter, any changes made will be automatically reflected for all users. If changes have been approved, please remember to post updates in the MCM CRIMS external chat for communication transparency.
If they are already in CR a popup will appear asking if they want to see the changes—users should click 'Yes' to confirm and view the latest updates. Additionally, users will need to close the CR and reopen it in order to view the updates.

1. Access Profile ManagerLog in to Charles River (CRD) using your admin account.
Open Profile Manager.
In the profile Preferences, set Show personalized profiles to Yes.

2. Select and Prepare the User’s ProfileIn Profile Manager, locate and select the specific profile assigned to the affected user.
Open the profile.
Under the Default Profile of Users section, add your admin user.
Under the Visible to Group section, add the SysAdmin group.
3. Save and Log OutSave the profile.
Log out of CRD completely, from all instances (QA, UAT and PROD) do not keep any instance open.
4. Clear Local CacheNavigate to the local CRD client folder: C:\Program Files (x86)\Charles River\21R2\client
Inside this directory, locate the folder with a long alphanumeric key (this is the cache folder).
Delete this cache folder.
5. Log Back In Log back in to CRD.
The user's profile should now load as your default profile, opening automatically.
6. Apply Fixes and Restore PermissionsMake the required profile adjustments (e.g., blotter settings, column layout, filters, etc.).
Save the profile again.
Remove your admin user from the User section.
Remove the SysAdmin group from the Group section.
Save the profile.
7. User ValidationInstruct the user to restart CRD.
When the prompt appears asking to reload the updated profile, tell them to click Yes and proceed.
Ask the user to verify that everything looks correct and functioning as expected.

This process should be used when a user’s CRD profile blotter becomes corrupted, missing, or not loading correctly, such as when columns disappear, filters fail, layouts reset, or the profile does not open as expected. Performing this procedure rebuilds the user’s profile by temporarily assigning it to an admin, resetting cached settings, applying the correct layout adjustments, and then restoring the profile back to the user. It is typically required after profile corruption, incomplete profile updates, backend configuration changes, or when the user reports that their blotter no longer reflects the intended setup.`

- `Resolution
The issue regarding trades flowing into Charles River (CR) with the incorrect custodian has been investigated. The problem was noted with trades that should clear at Citi but were incorrectly routed to GS.

 Examined the Clearing house field in CR and confirmed that trades are being recorded with the custodian GS instead of Citi.
 Identified that opening orders from TradeWeb have "clear broker" set to GSI PB, which flows into CR as GSS.
 Verified that for unwind trades, the custodian remains GSS based on the initiating clear broker setting.
 Provided examples of FIX messages to demonstrate that the issue originates from TradeWeb settings, where Citi was not selected across all currency pairs.
 Confirmed the resolution source was Runbook execution. No further steps required as per the provided guidance.

Verification

 Ensure all new trades flow into CR with the correct custodian (Citi).
 Review settings in TradeWeb to confirm that Citi is properly selected as the custodian across all currency pairs.
 Cross-check the FIX message logs for post-resolution trades for accuracy.

Next Steps/Prevention
To prevent recurrence, verify and update TradeWeb settings to ensure Citi is consistently selected for the appropriate trades. Regular monitoring of incoming trade data can help identify similar misconfigurations early.

Description of the Problem
Recently, trades have been observed to flow into the clearing system (CR) with an incorrect custodian assigned. This issue appears to specifically affect trades that should be clearing at Citi but are instead being routed to Goldman Sachs (GS). The occurrence of this misassignment has been noted over the past week or two and may be linked to data inputs or rule settings originating from the internal system referred to as "Mars."

The details provided point toward potential discrepancies or misconfigurations in custodian assignment rules, specifically within swap trades linked to the Clearing House field. Immediate investigation of the trades and rule configurations is required to identify the source of the error and ensure proper routing to Citi as intended.

Further clarification is needed regarding: the specific rule sets currently active in Mars, any recent changes made to custodian assignment, and whether other asset classes or processes are similarly impacted.

Original Request Example
"Can you guys look at something… seeing this in the last week or two… trades are flowing into CR with bad custodian… all these should clear at citi"`
- `ResolutionThe issue was caused by the WI bond Data Quality being incorrectly set as "New Security" instead of "New Issue." This caused calculation failures when performing fixed income analytics on BUONI POLIENNALI DEL TES 3.95 10/01/2041.
Corrective actions implemented to resolve the issue:
Updated the Data Quality field to "New Issue" for the WI bond.Replayed the FIX message, which succeeded after adjusting the Data Quality.VerificationEnsure the FIX message replay succeeds without errors.Confirm that the Data Quality field is correctly set to "New Issue" for similar WI bonds prior to their issue date.Next Steps/PreventionIt is recommended that all New Issue Bonds prior to their issue date are set to "New Issue" as the Data Quality setting to prevent calculation failures. Transition the Data Quality setting only after the issue date.

Description of the ProblemA Tradeweb FIX message appears to have failed to process properly into the Charles River system (CR). The specific error is related to fixed income calculations on a security, identified as BUONI POLIENNALI DEL TES 3.95 10/01/2041. The analytics for this bond type are unsupported when the settle date falls within an odd first coupon period. The sender has requested a replay of the message to address the issue.
The error details indicate a "recalc failed" issue and provide a FIX message extracted from Tradeweb with specific identifiers like FIXID "Q209BARXEU047551" and the security "Symbol" related to EUR transactions. Additional clarification may be needed to confirm whether the replay is feasible and whether further security creation is required.
Next steps should involve confirming the feasibility of replaying this message and identifying specific actions required for resolution.



Original Request Example"Hi @#OMSSupport – please check what the issue is with this Tweb message not feeding into CR. Can we replay this?"`
- `ResolutionThe issue with resizing the termed trade and canceling the existing repo has been resolved. Below are the steps taken to address the problem along with key observations:
Identified the error was related to an insufficient "Pledged" amount of the collateral bond.Tested in QA environment and found that booking a REPO Short increases the "Pledged" amount of the collateral bond.In QA, it was verified that a new repo short for 1mm increased the "Pledged" amount by 1mm. However, in PROD, the collateral had a Beginning of Day (BOD) and ledger value of 1mm, which caused inadequate pledged quantity.Confirmed with the user ("Danny") on the call about the repo order unable to be canceled.Successfully reverted changes to the collateral bond's position and booked a new repo trade for 1mm with the correct trade and close dates.Order was successfully sent to accounting.VerificationEnsure the pledged amount aligns correctly after booking a REPO Short.Confirm that the original 2mm portion of the repo has been canceled completely.Check with accounting that all trades have been successfully processed.Next StepsIf further adjustments or validations are required related to pledged quantities or repo order cancellations, please reach out for support.

Description of the ProblemA user from Moore Capital Management, LLC is facing an issue while trying to cancel a repo trade that was already done in the market. The user reports encountering an error during the cancellation process for an original trade piece amounting to 2 million. Additionally, the new trade for today has already been sent to accounting. Resolution of the cancellation issue is required to complete this trade adjustment.
Exact details concerning the error message encountered during the cancellation attempt are not provided. Please specify the exact error message received to facilitate troubleshooting. Additionally, any workflow dependencies or system configurations related to the termed trade cancellation should be shared.


Original Request ExampleHi – I’m trying to resize a termed trade for today. Trade has been done in the market, but when I try to cancel the existing repo, I’m getting the following error: The new trade has been sent to accounting already, just waiting on the 2mm original piece to be cancelled.`

Transition18 high priority nightly job support will be transitioned in Phase I

- `Scenario 1: Job Failed Alert in Halo Summary: We typically receive an alert in Halo at 4:00 AM ET / 12:00 PM Dubai time . Upon checking, the job appears to be still running and hasn’t completed. Immediate escalation is required to avoid delays in the Start of Day (SOD). Best Practice: Proactively check this job by 2:00 AM ET / 10:00 AM Dubai time to identify issues earlier. Early detection helps prevent SOD delays. Action Steps: Upon receiving the alert: Confirm the job status in AutoSys (it may appear as running). As per Jay’s instructions, call him immediately . If Jay is unreachable: Attempt to manually rerun the job in AutoSys: Right-click on the failed job. Select "Force Start" from the dropdown menu. Click "Go" . Confirm the action in the pop-up window by clicking "OK" . If the job fails again: Send an email to the DBA team , and cc Jay and Tristian . Attach the alert email received from Jennison regarding the job failure. If there is still no response: Call the TOC Hotline . If no one responds, escalate further: Tristian may reach out to TSG and DBA via message. Request confirmation that they’re receiving the alerts. Ask if someone can directly call a DBA team member using their personal contact. Resolution Example: Jay did not respond, and emails to the DBA team went unanswered. Attempts to reach the TOC Hotline were unsuccessful. Tristian escalated to TSG and DBA teams, requesting they confirm receipt of emails and contact a DBA directly via phone. The delay was due to a lack of timely response from the client side. Scenario 2: Job Running Longer Than Expected Summary: Job did not fail, but continued running for over an hour , although it typically completes within one minute . Action Steps: Check the job’s status and duration in AutoSys. Note the job’s start time and expected completion time. If the job runs unusually long (i.e., beyond its typical duration): Notify Jay and Tristian. Monitor closely for any changes or signs of failure. If no update after a reasonable wait (e.g., 1 hour), escalate to the DBA team and provide details. Include logs or screenshots if possible.

- `See related ticket: https://agent.omnivista.com/ticket?id=27065

Steps to Update the Record in CRD_ARCHIVE
1. Log in to the database server:
Connect to the SQL-CRD server using the CRDADMIN account (password: moore2007).
2. Check the existing record in the CRD_ARCHIVE database:
Run the following query in the CRD_ARCHIVE database:

Note the current values returned for the record.


3. Check the corresponding record in the CRD database:
Run the same query in the CRD (active) database.
Compare the results with the CRD_ARCHIVE version.

You will likely see that the record exists in both databases but with different details (e.g., the record TEMPLATE9 appears with updated information).


4. Update the archived record to reflect the changes:
You may choose to either:
Rename a column (e.g., update the INIT field to add _old), or
Fully synchronize the record with the version in the CRD database.
Example update:


5. Retry the archival process:
Return to the archival page.
Rerun the archival step that previously failed.
The process should now complete successfully without encountering a duplicate error.

BackgroundOccasionally, when a user updates the details of an existing record, the archival process encounters a conflict. This happens because the record already exists in the CRD_ARCHIVE database, and the archival job attempts to insert it again, resulting in a duplicate error.

To resolve this, you can either:
Delete the existing record from the CRD_ARCHIVE database, or
Update the record in the CRD_ARCHIVE database to reflect the current changes.`

- `Server Stop/Start Order
When performing this procedure:
All servers must be stopped before starting any of them again.
Stop them in the following order:
us2uat-cr03
us2uat-cr04
us2uat-cr05
us2uat-cr06
us2qa-cr01
us2qa-cr02
Once all servers have been fully stopped, restart them in the same order listed above.⚠️ Important: Do not start any server until you have confirmed that all servers have been stopped.

Procedure
1) Login to passwordstate, we will use us2uat-cr04 as an example.

2) UAT → us2uat-cr04.win.moorecap.com → Auto Launch


3) Open Service → Right click on SMS Agent → Properties → from Automatic to Manual → Apply → click ‘Ok’ → then click Stop






4) Right click → Stop (If there are two instances of the server, make sure to stop US2UATCR048081 first and then US2UATCR048082) 


5) Go back to WebAdmin and see if the servers have been inactive.

 
6) Once the servers have been inactive click on the windows button on the bottom left of the screen and click on restart to restart the machine, give it a few minutes after restarting and then bring the servers back on. 



7) Once all servers have been stopped and restarted, begin starting them again in the same order:us2uat-cr03 → us2uat-cr04 → us2uat-cr05 → us2uat-cr06 → us2qa-cr01 → us2qa-cr02.
⚠️ Important: After starting each server, check WebAdmin to confirm it is fully active before moving on to the next server.

In case we have two instances of a server, make sure to start US2UATCR048081 first and then US2UATCR048082 by right click → start. 


8) Double check WebAdmin to see if the servers are up and active. Then start SMS Agent Host

This document provides the step-by-step procedure for rebooting UAT servers, including stopping services, restarting the machine, and verifying activity. The example below uses us2uat-cr04 as the reference.

Why We Do This

As of September 5 2025, the Tidal jobs and restart scripts are not running correctly. While we wait for a permanent resolution, this manual reboot process is followed in the UAT environment to ensure servers and services are restarted properly.

We also perform daily UAT checks around 10:00 AM DXB time (GMT+4) / 2:00 AM EST. For these checks to succeed, it is critical that all UAT services and servers are restarted in the correct order. This procedure ensures stability until the automated jobs and scripts are fixed.`

- `sFTP: Secure File Transfer Protocol Commands: bash: Starts a new Bash shell session. cd bin: Changes current directory to a bin directory. ls: Lists the content of the current directory. cat: Display the contents of a file in the terminal. Patrick Pingaro usually asks if he can proceed to step 2, this can verified using this page: https://agent.omnivista.com/kb?id=61

Open the job in question (in Tidal) to check which .bat fils it is executing. Locate the job in question. Open job details (through right clicking on job).In the Override tab, under the Command section, we can see the .bat file the job is executing.Open PasswordState, and navigate to the Recon folder found in the menu on the left.Navigate to K Recon folder (shortcut found on Desktop), open FTP folder.Locate the .bat file noted in step 1. Right click and select edit. Select the highlighted statement below, stop after "drop-off" and copy. NOTE: Copy from beginning of line and not from the "plink". some files have a path before which should be copied (check 1 images below - showing that path should be also copied)Open Command window, paste the copied plink statement from step 5 and press enter.Type the below command in order and separately: (Each command's purpose is in the internal memo section at the page's end) bashcd binlsOpen the folder mentioned at the end of the Plink statement (highlighted below), by executing the following command: cat FolderName (Ex: cat GetDcCh.sh)This will display the script in GetDcCh.sh, which contains the username and url of the FTP site where the Prime Broker's files are being delivered.We will now access the site where the files are being uploaded to check if they are available using the below command:/usr/local/bin/sftp “Username@websiteURL" - The username and URL are found in the script and change depending on which script we are looking at (Step 9, highlighted).Ex for this case: /usr/local/bin/sftp moorepb@securefiletransfer.citigroup.comType in the below two commands to view received files:cd outgoinglsThis should show available files in the ftp.Example of case where files were missing (Ticket 23684):Example of case where files were found: (We were looking for the file labeled 29th April)In the case where files were not found in FTP:Send an email out to Prime Brokers, mentioned in the initial alert, requesting they send the missing files and including a screenshot of the files from the ticket. Contact emails of Prime Brokers that should be contacted are available in this page. CC Patrick Pingaro, Mark Buckthorp and Gina DeRosa in the email. (Make sure when copying the missing files table to crop out the first file bony_pos.txt)Communicate with Patrick through the ticket (email), informing him that the files are missing on the prime broker's side and that you reached out to them by email.If no response is received from prime brokers within 30 minutes, try reaching out by calling if a number is available (some prime brokers sent automated email at first, which might include their phone numbers). Also, escalate in App Support External teams chat too, they might be able to reach out to prime broker from their side and let them know you sent out an email and included them already.Once notified that files were sent or being able to locate them in FTP, rerun the associated tidal job (this will pull the files into C Prime Broker folder).Go into the folder, like initial steps, to verify missing files can now be located with correct dates in the folder.Make a copy of the files that were missing from C Prime Broker folder to Z Prime Broker folder (shortcut also available on desktop).If files are found in FTP, they now need to be pulled manually using the steps in Pulling Files Manually from RECON (confluence page until we move it to KB).Escalate to Zahraa Awad if this couldn't be resolved with the available steps or an issue was encountered.

In this step, we will log in to the appropriate sFTP to check if the files are present where they are expected to be. This step is usually carried out after the files couldn't be located in the initial checks. 

This helps verify whether the files exist in the source location before taking further action. If files are found in FTP, this would indicate they're missing from Prime Broker's side.`

- `Step 1: Initial Status Check
Verify the status of the Message Broadcaster in Web Admin and open the Support mailbox simultaneously.
If the broadcaster is Active on us1-crd078081 (primary) or us1-crd088081 (backup), notify the MCM CRIMS Support – External chat that the alert has been received and the broadcaster is currently active. Also note that you will continue to monitor it's status. 
If the status is Not Running, notify the MCM CRIMS Support chat immediately and proceed to investigate further.
Message Broadcaster not running screenshot here

Step 2: Consistent Communication
Maintain regular communication with the external Moore chat, providing updates every few minutes if the broadcaster remains down, if the root cause is still being investigated, or what actions are being taken

Scenarios for Investigation:

Scenario 1: CTM Blocking
If you find blocking in the Support mailbox, identify whether the SPIDs with a Blocker value of 0 are CTM related or not (captured below). If they are proceed with the following:
Access the prod database using the CRDADMIN login and terminate the blocking SPIDs.
Escalate to the following team members if you do not have prod CRDADMIN credentials:
US: Andrew, Anuja
Dubai: Raed, Mike, Mark (Moore)


Scenario 2: Non-CTM Blocking
If the command is not a CTM command, escalate to Andrew and/or Moore contacts (Maher or Prasad) for confirmation on what the process is before killing it.
Commands that should not be killed without explicit approval include (but are not limited to):
TRIG_TS_ORDER_FIRM_POSITION
TS_ORDER_AU
Fn_Append_Allocation_Fields
Broadcast Queries
Add new examples here


Scenario 3: No Blocking but Long-Running Transactions
Navigate to Web Admin > Monitor and the Support mailbox to identify any existing long-running transactions.
If the transaction is broadcast query related, it should be killed.
Broadcast queries can be killed in the case of long-running transactions vs. a blocking because as a long running transaction, the process has not committed yet.
If unrelated, escalate to Andrew, Maher, or Prasad to get confirmation if the process can be terminated.


Scenario 4: No Blocking, No Long-Running Transactions
Review the Message Logs and Server Logs for any errors.
If errors are found, resolve appropriately (examples include restarting services, reboot middle tier, or adjust broadcast queries with Maher).
If no errors are found, initiate a failover to the backup middle tier.
Include steps here
Need to include a screenshot of server logs with errors

What is Message Broadcaster
Moore has the Message broadcaster which passes information to their external Process Server. This external process server expects a specific schema. That schema not being present means it will ignore the extraneous messages. Meaning if the Broadcaster isn’t configured properly the messages won’t be consumed at all and will not throw an error.

Alert: Msg Broadcaster Down or Inactive
If you receive the alert below indicating that the message broadcaster is down, and you find that the Message Broadcaster is inactive, please follow the steps outlined below to address the issue.

Why Are We Getting These Alerts
Msg Broadcaster alert is configured based on the heartbeat being stale by a minute. Ongoing major system activity has been shown to impact the heartbeat just as much as the broadcaster itself choking on CTM or its own queries. General system slowness has been also reported.`

- `Terms used: Manager refers to TaxLot Managers that have their own manager codes. This is defined in OA.TAX_LOT UDF_CHAR1 of the User Defined Fields > Allocation. UUID is the Bloomberg ID for users that is unique, and is required to permission users for BBGFIT for VCON

The first thing to do is to check where the mappings for the connection are. For VCON, we know it is BBGFIT, so opening the broker window and checking what is mapped in the broker details will determine where the permissions need to be adjusted for users that need to be added.
Open the broker window and check in the FIX Parameters section for hints: As BBGFIT - VCON is unsolicited, we check the unsolicited section and notice that trader and account are referencing the BBGFIT which can be found in the Import/Export Translations table.We can also see that the tag map is also in the same translation table BBGFIT:The above two findings indicate that we need to look at the BBGFIT external system for accounts and traders, and then check the BBGFIT FIX_TAG table for more information for the manager.For the TRADER table, we need to check if the user is added, and if not, then we have to add.for the Account, as there are two, we can check what table is needed by opening the FIX_TAG and checking the account related rows: we can see it checks the BBGFIT_USR_ACCT_MAP table so if user is not added there, they need to be added.The third step is to determine where the Manager mappings need to be added. Next section walks through this process.

Determining if a user is a manager requires us to do the following, if we do not confidently know if the user is one or not:
From the User Profile, we can see the groups that the user is part of. By checking these groups (primary group mainly) we will determine if there is a dedicated manager group for the user. In this case, checking primary group in detail does not indicate that user is manager.Check the Manager (TL) table in User Defined Fields shown below (under allocation) for the expected Manager Code - in our example RXBThe third place we can check is in the import/export tables > MCM_MAPS and Data_Item = Manager. Check of expected code is there. If existing user, and they are manager, it should be there.If the three checks above are consistently indicating that a user is or is not a manager, then we can proceed with this in mind that for VCON that user needs to be added. If they are a Manager, then based on the findings from the Broker FIX Parameters, and the FIX_TAG for BBGFIT tables, we know we have to add the manager to the BBGFIT_USR_MGR_MAP table in import/export translations:



Once these are done, then the permissions should be correct and can proceed to communicate with requestor.

Users at Moore use VCON frequently, and new users keep being added regularly. Understanding where to do changes to grant new users permissions for VCON is critical.`
